scheme_name: Housing Subsidy Scheme (GBOCWWB)
details:
The “Housing Subsidy Scheme” is implemented by the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board (GBOCWWB), Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. The main objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to construction workers registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board to build their own houses.
            In order to provide financial relief to the construction workers by giving subsidies for the houses purchased by them and to provide social stability to the construction workers, the  "Housing Subsidy" schemewasn started in the year 2021-22. It is planned to provide financial relief and social stability to construction workers through providing subsidies.
benefits:
Under the scheme, the assistance of ₹1,50,000/- will be provided to registered construction workers for a duration of 5 years, with ₹30,000/- disbursed annually subject to the terms of assistance.
The construction workers who have taken a loan up to a maximum of ₹15 lakhs to buy their house will be provided ₹30,000/- per year for the five years as per plan.
eligibility:
The construction workers registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
At the time of applying for the benefits of this scheme, it will be mandatory to have at least the last 2 years of registration with the construction worker's board.
To avail the benefit of this scheme the construction worker should have documented the building after the date of resolution of the scheme (29/06/2020). The application has to be made within one year from the date of the document mentioned in the house purchase document.
The loan taken for a house purchased by a registered construction worker should not exceed ₹15 lakhs and the loan tenure should be 10 years or more.
The registered construction worker benefiting from this scheme should not own any house or property in the joint name of the worker or his wife or joint, which guarantee has to be given by the worker in an affidavit as per the prescribed format herein.
The registered construction worker has to submit the bank loan interest certificate/bank loan statement after the completion of the financial year to avail the assistance under the Housing Subsidy Scheme.
The registered construction worker receiving benefits under this scheme has to submit a written proposal every year at the district office of the concerned board to get the remaining four years of assistance after receiving the assistance for the first time along with a copy of the in-principle approval letter and the annual statement of interest.

scheme_name: Award to Scheduled Castes Students Who Secured Top Ranks at State and District Level in Standard X & XII (Sahuji Maharaj Assistance)
details: The scheme “Award to Scheduled Castes Students Who Secured Top Ranks at State and District Level in Standard X & XII” is initiated by the Director Scheduled Caste Welfare, Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat. Under the scheme, the Scheduled Castes Category students who have Secured Top Ranks at the State and District Levels in Gujarat in Standard X & XII are given cash prizes. Cash prizes are awarded to the first three students who secure the highest ranks in both Standard X and Standard XII across all streams.
benefits:
Award/Cash Prizes Details at State Level:
Sl. No.	Student’s Rank	Award for Standard X	Award for Standard XII (For All Stream)
1	First	₹41,000/-	₹31,000/-
2	Second	₹21,000/-	₹21,000/-
3	Third	₹11,000/-	₹11,000/-
Award/Cash Prizes Details at District Level:
Sl. No.	Student's Rank	Award for Standard X	Award for Standard XII (For All Stream)
1	First 	₹6,000/-	6,000/-
2	Second	₹5,000/-	5,000/-
3	Third	₹4,000/-	4,000/-
eligibility:
The students should belong to the Scheduled Castes Category of Gujarat.
The students who have Secured Top Ranks at the State and District Levels in Gujarat in Standard X & Standard XII across all streams are eligible for cash prizes.
There is no income limit.
application_process:
Offline
Under this scheme, the list of students is obtained from the Gujarat Secondary and Higher Secondary Board and the award is given to the selected students accordingly.

scheme_name: Coaching Assistance to Scheduled Castes Students for Preparation of Examination Like IIM, NIFT, NLU, CEPT
details: The scheme “Coaching Assistance to Scheduled Castes Students for Preparation of Examination Like IIM, NIFT, NLU, CEPT” was launched by the Director Scheduled Caste Welfare, Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat. Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to Students of the Scheduled Castes category who are preparing for examinations like IIM, NIFT, NLU, and CEPT.
benefits: Under this scheme, ₹20,000/- or the actual coaching fee to be paid, whichever is less.
eligibility:
Eligibility Criteria for Student:
The students should belong to Scheduled Caste Category.
A student should have secured 70% or more in Class 10th and studying in Class 12th (any stream) to get admission in CEPT, NIFT, or NLU.
To get admission in IIMs: Students should have secured 70% or more in Class 12th (any stream) and be in the third year of graduation (any stream) or should have secured 70% or more marks in graduation if completed graduation.
There is no income limit.
Eligibility Criteria for Institute:
The organization should have more than three years of experience.
The organization should have a GST Number/PAN card.
Institutions should have biometric (fingerprint) machines for student attendance.
The training institute should be registered under any one of the following Acts.
Mumbai Public Trust Act, 1950
Companies Act, 1956
Shop and Establishment Act, 1948 (Shops and Institutions Act, 1948)

scheme_name: Dr. P. G. Solanki Scheme for Loan/Assistance to Law Graduates of Scheduled Caste
details: The scheme “Dr. P. G. Solanki Scheme for Loan/Assistance to Law Graduates of Scheduled Caste” was launched by the Director Scheduled Caste Welfare, Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat. The scheme was launched in the year 1973. Under this scheme, law graduates belonging to the Scheduled Caste who intend to start independent legal practice will receive financial assistance for the purchase of business premises/shops.
benefits:
Under the scheme, A Law graduates belonging to Scheduled Caste intending to start legal practice will be given ₹12,000/-; out of which ₹7,000/- will be @4% interest loan, and ₹5,000/- will be a subsidy.
The loan is provided to purchase business premises/shops.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a Law graduate.
The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
The benefit of this scheme will be given only to natives of Gujarat state.
The annual family income of the applicant limit should not exceed ₹6,00,000/- for rural areas and ₹6,00,000/- for urban areas.
Note: Registration number and date received from Bar Council (to be attached with attested copy of registration certificate).

scheme_name: Coaching Assistance Scheme for Preparation of Recruitment Exams for Scheduled Caste Students
details: The scheme “Coaching Assistance Scheme for Preparation of Recruitment Exams for Scheduled Caste Students” was launched by the Director Scheduled Caste Welfare, Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat. The scheme was launched during the year 2017-18. Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to Students of the Scheduled Castes category who are preparing for all State Government and Central Government Recruitment Examinations of Class– 1, 2 & 3 conducted by UPSC/GPSC/State Commission/Bank/LIC/Subordinate Services Selection Board/District Panchayat Selection Board.
benefits:
Under this scheme, ₹20,000/- or the actual fee to be paid, whichever is less.
The assistance amount is directly credited to the student's bank account through DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer).
eligibility:
Eligibility Criteria for Student:
The students should belong to Scheduled Castes Category.
The student should have passed the graduation exam with 50% or more marks.
The age limit will be a maximum of 35 years for males and a maximum of 40 years for females.
The applicant or the applicant's parents should not be in government employment.
The applicant will not be able to pursue full-time studies, job/business, etc. at any other place during the training.
The benefit of this scheme will be available to the applicant only once.
Note: After receiving the coaching, the details of which examination the student himself/herself has given should be sent from time to time to the concerned Deputy Director, Office of Scheduled Caste Welfare.
Eligibility Criteria for Institution:
The Institution should have more than three years of experience.
The Institution should have a GST Number/PAN Card.
Institutions should have biometric (fingerprint) machines for student attendance.
For competitive recruitment examinations, the faculty should have at least graduation and general knowledge.
A training institution should be registered under any one of the following Acts.
Mumbai Public Trust Act, 1950
Companies Act, 1956
Shops and Establishments Act, 1948

scheme_name: Assistance for Power Driven Chaff Cutter Scheme under Scheduled Castes Sub Plan (SC Category)
details: The scheme “Assistance for Power Driven Chaff Cutter Scheme under Scheduled Castes Sub Plan (SC Category)” is implemented by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat. Under this scheme, financial assistance is given to dairy farmers belonging to the Scheduled Caste category on purchase of electric powered chaff cutters. By using power driven chaff cutter, dairy farmers can reduce wastage of green and dry fodder which is economically important for their profitable dairy farming.
benefits:
Under the scheme, financial assistance up to 75% of the purchase cost of powered chaff cutter or ₹18,000/-, whichever is less, is given to beneficiaries of Scheduled Caste category.  
Note: The amount of assistance will be deposited in bank account of the beneficiary.
eligibility:
The beneficiary should be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
The beneficiary should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
The beneficiary should have a minimum of five (5) animals of his/her own.

Note: After sanctioning application through I-Khedut Portal, beneficiary has to purchase Chaff Cutter from government approved empanelled vendor and has to produce the bill for claim submission of subsidy.

scheme_name: Kisan Suryoday Yojana (KSY)
details: The "Kisan Suryoday Yojana (KSY)" was launched by the Energy and Petro Chemicals Department, Gujarat, in 2020-2021 with the goal of providing daytime electricity to farmers for agricultural activities. This innovative scheme aims to address the power demands of farmers by supplying electricity from 5:00 am to 9:00 pm. By maximizing the use of solar and non-conventional energy sources, the scheme ensures a reliable and eco-friendly power supply. The KSY is being implemented in phases, aligned with the operationalization of solar and alternative energy generation plants.
benefits:
Daytime Electricity Supply: Ensures uninterrupted electricity for agricultural activities during the day from 5:00 am to 9:00 pm. 
Sustainable Energy: Promotes the use of solar and non-conventional energy sources, reducing dependency on conventional power.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a farmer or engaged in agricultural activities.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: Visit the nearest electricity board office.
Step 2: Obtain the application form for the scheme.
Step 3: Fill out the form with accurate details and attach the required documents.
Step 4: Submit the application online or at the designated office.
Step 5: Upon verification, receive updates on the approval status and the power connection schedule.

Proof of land ownership.
Recent passport-sized photographs.
Any other documents (if required).

scheme_name: Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for assistance to MSMEs: Net SGST reimbursement to Micro, Small and Medium Manufacturing Enterprise (MSMEs):
details:
The "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat Scheme for MSMEs" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state's dynamic MSME sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from October 5, 2022, to October 4, 2027, this initiative supports growth, investments, and the vision of "Aatmanirbhar Gujarat" and "Aatmanirbhar Bharat."
The component "Net SGST reimbursement to Micro, Small and Medium Manufacturing Enterprise (MSMEs)" provides reimbursement of net SGST based on the taluka category of the project.
benefits:
Category of Taluka 	Quantum of Incentive 
 Category 1	100% of net SGST for 10 years upto 7.5% of eFCl p.a. 
Category 2 	90% of net SGST for 10 years upto 6.5% of eFCl p.a. 
Category 3 & Municipal Corporation Areas	80% of net SGST for 10 years upto 5% of eFCI p.a. 
﻿

Note: The period of 10 years shall be from the date of commencement of commercial production.
eligibility:
The firm must be a Micro/Small/Medium enterprise.
The enterprise must have obtained acknowledgment or registration from the Ministry of MSME or the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), as applicable.
New manufacturing enterprises or existing ones undertaking expansion or diversification are eligible.
The enterprise must commence commercial production during the scheme's operative period.
The subsidy is based on the lower of the Gross Fixed Capital Investment (GFCI) or the cost appraised by a bank or financial institution.
Enterprises must obtain separate GST registration for manufacturing eligible products.
Separate books of accounts are required for each manufacturing unit, certified by a Chartered Accountant.
Incentives are only for goods manufactured in the eligible enterprise's premises, not for resold goods.
Compliance with all reporting and reimbursement requirements is mandatory.
application_process:
Online
Registration Process:
Step 1: Visit the Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP) portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form.
Step 2: Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit". 
Step 3: Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID.
Step 4: After verification, you will be registered successfully.
﻿

Post-Registration Process:
Step 1: Visit the Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP) portal. Click on "Login".
Step 2: Enter the email address and password you used during registration.
Step 3: Click the "Login" button to access your account.
Step 4: Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.
﻿

Application for Provisional/Final Eligibility Certificate for Net SGST: After commencement of commercial production, the eligible enterprise, shall submit an application for provisional eligibility certificate within 1 year from the date of commencement of commercial production or within one year from the date of issue of this G.R., whichever is later.
Procedure for claim of assistance for Net SGST: The eligible enterprise shall submit the claim application for assistance, at the interval of every 3 months, to Sanctioning & disbursing authority.
Helpdesk: Contact here.

scheme_name: Doodh Sanjivani Scheme
details: The "Doodh Sanjivani Scheme" launched by the Women and Child Development Department, Gujarat, is a welfare initiative designed to improve the nutritional status of children and women in tribal and underdeveloped communities. The scheme benefits children aged 6 months to 6 years and pregnant and lactating mothers registered at Anganwadi centers. It covers 138 tribal and developing communities in 20 districts and is implemented through Anganwadi centers.
benefits:
Nutritional Support for Children: 100 ml of fortified flavored milk is provided 5 days a week to children aged 6 months to 6 years.
Nutritional Support for Mothers: 200 ml of fortified flavored milk is provided 2 days a week to pregnant and lactating mothers.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a child aged 6 months to 6 years or a pregnant or lactating mother.
The applicant must be registered at an Anganwadi center.

Step 3: Provide the required documents.
Step 4: Avail of the benefits during scheduled distribution days.

scheme_name: Foreign Education Scheme
details: The "Foreign Education Scheme" introduced by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Gujarat, is designed to support meritorious students from economically weaker sections who wish to pursue higher education abroad. The financial assistance helps cover tuition fees, living expenses, and other educational costs. The repayment structure is flexible, ensuring students can repay the loan after completing their studies. The scheme is being implemented by the Gujarat Unreserved Education and Economical Development Corporation.
benefits:
Financial Assistance: A loan of the total tuition fee of the entire course or ₹15,00,000/-, whichever is less, will be provided by the Corporation at a simple interest of 4%.
Collateral:
For loans of total loan amount of ₹7,50,000/- or less for the entire course, a charge of one and a half times the loan amount will have to be registered on the beneficiary's own property or that of a relative. 
If the total loan amount for the entire course is more than ₹7,50,000/-, the immovable property of oneself or any other relative equal to that total amount will have to be mortgaged in favor of the Corporation.
Loan repayment:
In case of total loan up to ₹5,00,000/-, it will have to be repaid in equal monthly installments over 5 (five) years, 1 year after completion of studies.
In case of loan exceeding ₹5,00,000/-, it will have to be repaid in equal monthly installments over 6 (six) years, starting 1 year after completion of studies.
The money from the loan being repaid will continue to be deposited as interest first.
The borrower can repay the loan even before the specified time limit.
eligibility:
The applicant must belong to a unreserved category.
The applicant must have scored 60% or more in Standard 12.
The applicant must have secured admission to an MBBS, graduation, post-graduation, or equivalent diploma course abroad.
The applicant’s family's annual income must be ₹6,00,000/- or less.

scheme_name: Late Jaswantsinhji Thakor and Late Rasiklal Andhariya Classical Music Scholarship
details: The scheme "Late Jaswantsinhji Thakor and Late Rasiklal Andhariya Classical Music Scholarship" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to preserve and promote classical music in Gujarat by offering financial support to eligible Gurus for imparting training and to their disciples for receiving it. The scheme facilitates a 10-month classical music training period where selected Gurus can train up to 2 disciples, and both Gurus and disciples receive monetary assistance. The scheme is implemented by Gujarat Rajya Sangeet Natak Akademi, Gandhinagar, which releases a public advertisement annually inviting applications.
benefits:
For Gurus:
Financial assistance of ₹1,000/- per month for a duration of 10 months (i.e., ₹10,000/- per disciple) for training up to 2 disciples.
For Disciples:
A scholarship of ₹10,000/- per annum, calculated at ₹1,000/- per month for 10 months, for receiving classical music training from the selected Guru.
eligibility:
For Gurus:
The applicant should be a classical music artist.
The applicant should have completed formal education in classical music or must possess at least 7 years of experience in teaching classical music.
The applicant should be capable of training a maximum of 2 disciples during the 10-month training period.
For Disciples:
The applicant should be a student of classical music residing in Gujarat.
The applicant should be undergoing training under an eligible Guru selected under this scheme.
The applicant should submit a certificate of training provided by the Guru.
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Applications are invited once every year through a public advertisement by Gujarat Rajya Sangeet Natak Akademi.
Step 2: Applicants must submit the prescribed application form along with required documents to the designated address.
Step 3: The selection committee evaluates the applications and selects eligible Gurus and disciples.
Step 4: Both Guru and disciple must submit a report of training conducted and received over 10 months.
Step 5: Upon successful verification, scholarship amounts are disbursed directly to the beneficiary accounts.

scheme_name: Small Business (GBCDC)
details: The scheme "Small Business (GBCDC)" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance to the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) for undertaking small businesses like grocery store, dairy parlour, animal fodder sales centre, mobile-computer repairing shop, etc., for casual business. Through this scheme, a loan amount is provided at a low interest rate to promote self-employment among SEBC individuals across the state. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat Backward Classes Development Corporation (GBCDC). Beneficiaries can apply online without any application fee through the official GBCDC portal.
benefits:
Financial assistance in the form of a loan up to ₹2,00,000/-.
The loan can be utilised for starting small businesses such as grocery stores, dairy parlours, animal fodder sales centres, mobile and computer repairing shops, etc.
eligibility:
The applicant should belong to a socially and educationally backward class.
The applicant's total annual individual/family income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.

Online

Step 2: On the top menu bar, click on "Apply Online".
Step 3: If you’re a new user, click on "New Registration" Enter personal details (name, mobile number, email, aadhaar number, etc.), create a password, and complete OTP verification.
Step 4: Use your registered credentials (User ID and password) to log in.
Step 5: Click on “New Application”. Select “Small Business Scheme” from the list of available schemes.
Step 6: Fill out the Application Form, enter the following details:
Personal Information: Name, gender, date of birth, caste, etc.
Address Information: Permanent and correspondence address
Income Details: Monthly family income and source
Loan Requirement: Amount needed, proposed use (e.g., business, self-employment)
Bank Account Details: For loan disbursement
Step 7: Upload scanned copies of the Caste certificate, Income certificate, Aadhaar card, Passport-size photo, Bank passbook (first page), and Residence proof.
Step 8: Double-check all details. Click on “Preview” to verify. If all details are correct, click “Submit”.
Step 9: Applicant will receive an Application ID/Reference Number. Save or print the acknowledgement for future tracking.

scheme_name: Mahila Samruddhi (Gujarat Backward Caste Development Corporation)
details: The scheme "Mahila Samruddhi" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide a small-scale credit scheme for creating self-employment for women from backward classes. Through this scheme, low-interest rate loans are provided to female beneficiaries, enabling them to undertake a business of their choice. This loan is to be repaid in 48 equal monthly installments, including interest.
eligibility:

The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should belong to a socially and educationally backward class, specifically Other Backward Classes (OBC).
The total annual income of the applicant's family should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
The applicant's age should be between 21 and 45 years on the date of application.
The applicant must have experience in a business requiring technical and other skills.

Income Details: Monthly family income and source
Loan Requirement: Amount needed, proposed use (e.g., business, self-employment)

Step 7: Upload scanned copies of the Caste certificate, Income certificate, Aadhaar card, Passport-size photo, Bank passbook (first page), and Residence proof.

scheme_name: Transportation Scheme (Gujarat Gopalak Vikas Nigam)
details: The scheme "Transportation Scheme" by the Gujarat Gopalak Vikas Nigam, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide loans for the purchase of auto rickshaws and loading vehicles. Through this scheme, financial assistance for vehicle purchase is offered to the Rabari and Bharwad castes from the socially and educationally backwards classes. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat Gopalak Vikas Nigam.
benefits:
Loans are provided on an individual basis to purchase auto rickshaws and loading vehicles.
Maximum Loan Limit: ₹2,00,000/-
Interest Rate: 6% per annum
Loan Amount: 95% of the vehicle cost
Beneficiary Contribution: 5% of the vehicle cost
eligibility:
The applicant should belong to the Rabari and Bharwad castes from the socially and educationally backwards classes.
The applicant's age should be between 21 and 45 years.
The applicant's annual income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Register on the Official Portal
Go to the Official Website Gujarat Gopalak Development Corporation.
Scroll down on the homepage, find the "Transportation Scheme," and click on "Apply Now".
You will be directed to the application form. 
Step 2: Access and Fill Out the Application Form
Select "Transport", enter Unit Cost, and specify the Required Loan Amount.
Provide Aadhaar, Ration, Voter ID, full name (Last, First, Father/Husband), address, district, taluka, village, PIN, date of birth, age (21-45 as of 14/08/2023), caste, subcaste, phone, mobile, email, gender, and marital status.
Enter income certificate number, issue date, issuing officer's name, total annual family income (up to ₹ 3,00,000/-), and parent/guardian's occupation.
If "Physically Handicapped," select "Yes" or "No". If you selected "Yes", provide the percentage (in %).
Indicate if any family member works in a government or private job. If "Yes," provide their Name, Designation, Place, and Salary details.
Select your Education Qualification: Uneducated, Educated, Graduation, Post Graduation, or Other Degree.
If you have taken a past loan, select "Yes" or "No". If you selected "Yes", provide loan details (date, amount, paid, remaining) and your account number.
Enter driving license number and expiry date. For passenger vehicles, select "Yes" or "No". If you selected "Yes", provide the base number. For transport vehicles, select "Yes" or "No". If you selected "Yes", provide the transport license number.
Provide the dealer's name, address, vehicle name, and vehicle price.
Provide bank account number, bank name, branch name, name as per account, account type (Saving/Current), MICR code, and IFSC code.
Step 3: Review and Final Submission
Carefully review each module. Use the preview option to examine the application before final submission. Fill out the provided "CAPTCHA" and Click on “Submit”.

scheme_name: New Swarnim Yojana (for women only) GNTDNT
details: The scheme "New Swarnim Yojana (For Women Only)" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to empower women of nomadic and denotified castes living below the poverty line to achieve economic upliftment by getting self-employment with dignity. Under this scheme, women beneficiaries will be able to do business/occupation of their choice. The Gujarat Nomadic and Denotified Tribes Development Corporation implement the scheme.
benefits:
The maximum loan limit is up to ₹2,00,000/-.
The interest rate will be 5% per annum.
Under this scheme, 100% loan of the business/occupation amount is provided.
The loan amount will consist of 95% National Corporation contribution, 5% State Government contribution and zero beneficiary contribution.
﻿

Repayment: The loan amount, including interest, will have to be repaid in 60 equal monthly instalments.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a woman from a nomadic or denotified caste.
The applicant's family annual income limit for income eligibility will be up to ₹3,00,000/- (with effect from 1/4/2018).
The applicant's age should be at least 21 years and not more than 50 years as on the date of application.
The applicant must have experience in a technical and skilled trade/business.
﻿

Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:
At least 50% of the total loan amount will be allocated for families having an annual income of up to ₹1,50,000/-.

Fill in details about the college/institution admitted to, university affiliation, course name, enrollment number, organisation address, website, course duration (in years and semester), and date of admission.
Specify who accredited the course/institution, their name, and address. Proof of accreditation must be submitted.
Select "Yes" or "No" for "Have you obtained admission through government-mandated admission?".
If staying in a hostel, provide the Hostel name, Hostel address, and Hostel contact number.
Fill in the requested loan amounts for Admission Fee, Tuition Fee, Examination Fee, Hostel Fee, Estimated cost of books/stationery, and Other fees.
List details of any physical assets owned (land, house, government property, NCC, LIC, KVP, etc.).
Indicate if anyone in the family works in a government/private sector job. If "Yes," provide Name, Designation, Place, and Salary details.
Fill in the Bank Account Number, select the Bank Name from the dropdown, provide the Branch Name, Name as per Account, select Bank Account Type (Saving/Current), and enter the MICR Code and IFSC Code.
Step 3: Review and Final Submission

scheme_name: Free Medical Assistance (Gujarat)
details: The scheme "Free Medical Assistance" by the Tribal Development Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance for expensive medical treatments to people of Scheduled Tribes. Through this scheme, monetary aid is provided to Scheduled Tribe individuals suffering from diseases like tuberculosis, cancer, leprosy, HIV/AIDS, sickle cell anaemia, and thalassemia, as well as for serious maternity cases and anaemia in women.
benefits:
Financial assistance is provided to Scheduled Tribe individuals for medical treatment. The revised rates, effective from April 1, 2024, are as follows:
﻿

S. No.	Name Of Disease	Current rate of assistance	Proposed Rate of Assistance
A Type:
1	In Serve Cases Of Labour	₹500/- per case	₹2,000/- per case
2	For Female Genital Mutilation	₹150/- per case	₹2,000/- per case
3	Decay	₹500/- every month until the pain is gone	₹2,000/- every month until the pain subsides
4	Leprosy	₹800/- per month until the pain is gone	₹2,000/- every month until the pain subsides
B Type:
1	Cancer	Until then, ₹1,000/- per month will cure the pain.	₹2,500/- every month until the pain subsides
2	For AIDS	₹1,000/- every month until the pain subsides	₹2,500/- every month until the pain subsides
3	Sickle cell anaemia	₹500/- every month until the pain is gone	₹2,500/- every month until the pain subsides
4	Thalassemia	₹500/- per patient every month until the pain is relieved.	₹2,500/- per patient every month until the pain is relieved.
﻿

Mode of Disbursement: The financial assistance will be disbursed through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT). For HIV/AIDS patients, the payment will be made by cheque.
Frequency of Disbursement: Monthly until the patient recovers from tuberculosis, cancer, leprosy, AIDS, sickle cell anaemia, and thalassemia. Per case for serious maternity cases and anaemia in women.
Validity: The assistance is provided until the patient recovers from specified diseases.
eligibility:
The applicant should belong to Gujarat.
The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe.
The applicant's family income must be less than or equal to ₹6,00,000/-.
For HIV/AIDS patients, the annual income limit is ₹27,000/- for rural areas and ₹35,000/- for urban areas.
application_process:

Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the Chief District Health Officer.

scheme_name: Gujarat Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Standard 1st to 10th
details: The scheme "Gujarat Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for Standard 1st to 10th" by the Tribal Development Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance for educational development to Scheduled Tribe students studying in Classes 1st to 10th. Through this scheme, scholarships are annually provided to boys, girls, and extremely backward students from Classes 1st to 10th.
benefits:
The scheme provides financial assistance in the form of scholarships. The amounts are as follows:
For Boys
Class 1 to Class 8: Annual scholarship of ₹750/-.
Class 9 to Class 10: Annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.
For Girls
Class 1 to Class 5: Annual scholarship of ₹750/-.
Class 6 to Class 10: Annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.
For Extremely Backwards Students
Class 1 to Class 8: Annual scholarship of ₹1,000/-.
Class 9 to Class 10: Annual scholarship of ₹1,500/-.
﻿

Mode of Disbursement: The benefits to the beneficiaries will be disbursed through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) method. 
Frequency of Disbursement: The scholarship is provided annually.

The applicant must be studying in Class 1st to 10th.

scheme_name: Artist Honor (Gaurav Award)
details:
The “Artist Honor (Gaurav Award)” scheme is implemented by the Gujarat State Lalit Kala Academy under the Sports, Youth and Culture Activities Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to recognize and honor artists who have made a lifetime contribution in the field of fine arts, specifically in painting, sculpture, and graphic arts, and have achieved outstanding accomplishments.
Every year, a total of nine (9) artists are honored under this scheme, three in painting, three in sculpture, and three in graphic arts with the Gaurav Award. This award has been conferred annually since 1964-65.
benefits:
Award Details:
Cash prize of ₹51,000/- per artist.
Copper plate, citation, and shawl presented during the award ceremony.
Exhibition of artworks held for 5–7 days at Ravishankar Raval Kala Bhavan, Ahmedabad.
Catalogue publication of Gaurav Award-winning artists and their art.
Number of Awards:
Total of 9 artists are honored every year.
3 in painting, 3 in sculpture, and 3 in graphic arts.
eligibility:
The artist must be a resident of the State of Gujarat.
The artist must be associated with the field of fine arts, specifically in Painting, Sculpture or Graphic Arts.
The artist must have made a lifetime contribution in their respective field.
The artist should have achieved outstanding accomplishments or the highest level of recognition in their art form.
The applicant must be active in the art community and have a recognized body of work.
application_process:
Offline
Application Process: Applications are invited annually through public advertisements (daily newspapers).
Step 1: The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Gujarat State Lalit Kala Academy and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Gujarat State Lalit Kala Academy. 
Post-Application Process:
Step 1: A committee reviews and shortlists applicants based on merit.
Step 2: The final selected artists are notified and invited to the award ceremony.
Step 3: Awards are distributed along with the art exhibition and catalogue release.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Establishment of Small RAS Culture System Gujarat
details:
The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland) with the component of Establishment of Small RAS  (with 1 tank of minimum 100m3/tank capacity 10t/crop) culture system" by the Department of Fisheries, Government of India, aims to increase inland fish production by promoting fish rearing in reservoirs and providing employment to individuals involved in inland fisheries activities.
Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided for setting up Small Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) units, enabling higher fish yield using minimal land and water resources. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
﻿

Objective: 
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries. 
Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits:
Beneficiaries can receive assistance for the Establishment of Small RAS (with 1 tank of a minimum 100m3/tank capacity 10t/crop) culture system.
For the general category, the standard of assistance will be 40% of the unit cost ₹7,50,000/unit or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
For women, Scheduled Caste (SC), and Scheduled Tribe (ST) categories, the standard of assistance will be 60% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
﻿

Mode of Disbursement: Assistance is paid through 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from State Governments.
Validity: The benefit can be reclaimed once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.
The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
﻿

Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

scheme_name: State Mountaineering Reward Scheme
details: The scheme "State Mountaineering Reward Scheme" was launched by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide awards in the field of mountaineering is in place to enthusiastically engage and inspire the youth of the state towards risky and adventurous activities like summiting, mountaineering and rock climbing and to honor and commend the youth of the state who have achieved exceptional achievements in the field of mountaineering. Under the scheme, a cash award, a memento, and a certificate will be awarded to each of the adventurers of the state every year for their outstanding achievements in the field of mountaineering.

The applicant should have climbed a height of 7,000 meters once or a height of more than 6,000 meters twice.
The climbing achievement of the applicant must be confirmed by the Director of the Indian Mountaineering Foundation (IMF).
The applicant should not be involved in any criminal activity.
*This award can also be given posthumously, for which the legal heir of the awardee will have to apply.

scheme_name: Promotion of Organic Farming under State Organic Policy
details: The scheme "Promotion of Organic Farming under State Organic Policy" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat aims to promote sustainable agricultural practices by encouraging farmers to adopt organic farming methods. The scheme provides financial support for the registration and certification processes conducted by the Gujarat Organic Products Certification Agency (GOPCA) and Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA)-approved agencies. It also offers input cost assistance per hectare, enabling farmers to reduce chemical usage and enhance soil health.
benefits:
Organic Farming Registration and Certification Assistance (GOPCA): The scheme provides 75% at-source assistance in cost to the farmer for registration and certification when undertaken through the Gujarat Organic Products Certification Agency (GOPCA).
Organic Farming Registration, Certification, and Transportation Assistance (APEDA-approved agencies): When certification is done through APEDA-approved organic certification agencies, the scheme offers 50% assistance in registration, certification, and transportation fees. The maximum benefit for certification support is limited to ₹2,000/- per hectare.
Input Assistance for Organic Farming: Farmers are also eligible for input assistance amounting to ₹5,000/- per hectare to promote organic farming practices.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a farmer. 
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should be engaged in organic farming activities.

scheme_name: Assistance to Farmers for Mini Tractor and Power Tiller in Farm Mechanization Program
details: The scheme “Assistance to Farmers for Mini Tractor and Power Tiller in Farm Mechanization Program” by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to improve agricultural productivity and reduce manual labor burden by providing financial assistance for the purchase of mini tractors and power tillers to farmers. scheme_name: Tool Assistance Scheme for Pottery Artisans
details: The "Tool Assistance Scheme for Pottery Artisans" by the Gujarat Matikam Kalakari & Rural Technology Institute, Industries and Mines Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial support to individual artisans for procuring essential tools like electric chalk and electric footmill, offering a substantial 75% subsidy on the tool cost. The scheme offers this significant subsidy, requiring a 25% beneficiary contribution, and ensures that assistance is provided solely in the form of instrumental support, rather than cash. The applicant must fulfill specific criteria, including being directly involved in pottery work, belonging to the Prajapati/Kumbhar caste or being another associated artisan, and falling within the prescribed age group of 18 to 50 years. The procurement of Tool under this scheme is managed by the Gujarat Rural Industries Marketing Federation (GRIMCO), and applications are accepted at the office of the institution as per the prescribed format.
benefits:
Provision of tools such as an electric chalk and an electric footmill.
75% subsidy on the cost of the tool.
The instrument assistance is to be used solely for the purpose for which it was given and for the potter's work.
The assistance will be given only in the form of instrumental assistance and not in cash.
﻿

*The beneficiary must submit 25% of their contribution amount to the office of the institution through a demand draft in the name of 'GMK & RTI' of an authorized bank on the day disclosed by the organization.
*The applicant must use the instrument assistance only for the purpose for which it has been given and for the matter involving the potter's work.
*If the applicant misuses the assistance, the amount of tool assistance given will be recovered by the revenue organization.
*During verification by an officer of the institution, if the instrument assistance is found not to have been used for the intended purpose, the amount of the instrument assistance given will be withdrawn.
*The benefit of resource support will be available to only one person per family.
*The delivery of the tool must be checked upon receipt, as no complaint of any kind of instrument will be taken into account after delivery.
eligibility:
The applicant should be directly involved in the business of the Prajapati/Kumbhar caste.
The applicant should be directly involved in pottery work or pottery.
The applicant will have to give a certificate of competent authority for the purpose of doing pottery along with the application form.
The applicant can be another artisan associated with the pottery business who does pottery, provided they give a certificate of meaning along with the application form.
The applicant's age should be between 18 and 50 years.
The applicant must be the only person in their family applying for the benefit of resource support.
﻿

Post-Selection Conditions
The applicant shall have to use the instrument assistance for the purpose for which the instrument assistance has been given, only for that purpose and for the matter involving the potter's work.
The applicant must ensure the instrument assistance is used as intended; otherwise, the assistance amount will be withdrawn by the institution.
application_process:
Online
Offline
Steps for New Registration
Step 1: Visit the eKutir portal. In the right pane of the landing page, click "For New Individual Registration, click Here".
Step 2: On the registration page, fill in all mandatory fields (marked with an asterisk): Full Name (in English), Aadhaar Card Number, Gender, Date of Birth, Mobile Number, E-mail, Password (confirm the password by typing it again). Fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Register".
Step 3: A confirmation pop-up will display the entered name, mobile number, and other details. If all the details are correct, click "Confirm" to proceed. If any information is incorrect, click "Cancel", make the necessary corrections, and then click "Register" again.
Step 4: Upon successful registration, the User ID and Password will be sent to your registered mobile number via SMS. If an email address has been provided, the credentials will also be sent via email.
﻿

Steps for Login Procedure and User Profile Update
Step 1: Visit the eKutir portal. In the right pane of the landing page (in the "Login to Portal" section), provide your User ID, Password, and the Captcha Code. Then click "Login".
Step 2: Upon first login, the user must complete the "User Profile" by providing personal and organisational details. All fields marked with an asterisk are mandatory. Some fields will already be pre-filled based on the information provided during registration.
*Most of these details can later be updated from the “View Profile” section, except for the fields initially filled during registration.
﻿

Steps for Scheme Application
Step 1: After logging in and completing the profile, the system displays schemes relevant to the user on the homepage. Click on the name of the scheme that you intend to apply for. You will be redirected to the online application form of the scheme. The application form is divided into multiple tabs. 
Step 2: In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. For each uploaded document, the corresponding document number must be entered wherever required. Click the “Save & Next” button to proceed to the subsequent tabs. 
Step 3: Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections. Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any) by ticking the designated checkbox.
Step 4: Clicking “Save & Next” in the last tab of the application form generates an application number, which is displayed on the screen. The applicant must record this number for future reference. If the applicant wishes to print the application, they may click the “Print Application” button.
*If you do not wish to continue with the application at that point, you may click "Cancel".
﻿

Application Printing and Modification
If the user wishes to print the application, they may click “Print Application.” 
If the application has been returned for revision, a “Modify” button will appear. The user can then edit the application and resubmit it.
﻿

Helpdesk/Grievance Redressal
Call 9909926280 / 9909926180.
Connect with the District Industries Centre (DIC) of your district.
﻿

Check Application Status
Visit the Application Status page Provide the Application Number of the scheme, and your Date of Birth. Then click "View Status".

scheme_name: Assistance to Scheduled Castes for Fisheries: Training of Scheduled Caste trainees Gujarat
details:
The scheme "Assistance to Scheduled Castes for Fisheries" with the component of "Training of Scheduled Caste Trainees" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to increase fish production and provide employment opportunities to Scheduled Caste (SC) people engaged in inland fisheries activities. Through this scheme, financial assistance in the form of a stipend is provided to SC beneficiaries undergoing fisheries training to enhance their knowledge and skills. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries, Government of Gujarat.
Objectives:
To increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to scheduled caste (SC) people involved in inland fisheries activities.
To boost fish farming or fishing activities in freshwater bodies by scheduled caste (SC) fish farmers and fishermen.
Fishing in reservoirs, aquaculture in ponds, and catch from rivers increase fish production and boost inland fisheries through scheduled caste (SC) fish farmers and fishermen.
The main objective is to provide employment opportunities to Scheduled Caste fishermen in the state and increase fish production.
benefits:
The trainee is paid a stipend of ₹1,250/- after completing ten days of training.
The stipend is paid at the rate of ₹125/- per day.
﻿

Training Duration: 10 days of fisheries training.
Frequency of Disbursement: Once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) community.
The applicant should be above 18 years of age.
The applicant should be involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant can be an individual, a society, a self-help group, an association, a reservoir monopoly, or a private unit.
The applicant must have a bank account in their own name.
The applicant must have an Aadhar Card/Voter ID/Ration Card/Passport as proof of identity.
﻿

Preference/Reservation:
The scheme specifically benefits Scheduled Caste fishermen under the inland fisheries scheme.

Offline

Step 3: Fill the Application Form completely with accurate details.
Step 4: Attach the Required Documents, including identity proof, SC certificate, and bank account proof.
Step 5: Submit the Application in person to the concerned official at the District Fisheries Office.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Nidan Yojana
details: Launched in 2015-16, the scheme "Mukhyamantri Nidan Yojana" (also known as "Free Laboratory Testing Scheme") by the Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide quality diagnostic facilities without charge at all government health institutions and ensure timely and accurate diagnosis of diseases like malaria, tuberculosis, cancer, diabetes, hypertension to reduce morbidity and mortality in communities. The scheme provides free essential pathology, biochemistry, hematology, microbiology testing services at all government health institutions and strengthens existing laboratory facilities with quality equipment and trained staff along with regular supply of reagent kits. The applicant must be seeking healthcare services at government health facilities and require diagnostic testing for medical conditions. The scheme is implemented through a three-tier healthcare structure consisting of Sub Health Centers, Primary Health Centers, Community Health Centers, Sub-District Hospitals, District Hospitals and Medical Colleges with a total budget allocation of ₹200.94 crores, and applications are accepted offline at respective government healthcare facilities.
benefits:
Primary Benefits
Free Laboratory Testing Services: All essential pathology, biochemistry, hematology, and microbiology tests provided without any charge.
Diagnostic Facilities: X-ray, sonography, and Electrocardiography services at Community Health Centers
Comprehensive Healthcare Coverage: Testing for communicable and non-communicable diseases, maternal and child health conditions.
Service Delivery
Timely Diagnosis: Early detection of diseases like malaria, tuberculosis, cancer, diabetes, hypertension.
Quality Equipment: Modern diagnostic equipment and trained laboratory technicians.
Regular Supply: Continuous availability of reagent kits and testing materials.
Accessibility: Services available at doorstep through Sub Health Centers, Primary Health Centers, Community Health Centers, Sub-District Hospitals, District Hospitals, and Medical College hospitals.
Implementation Structure
Total Budget: ₹200.94 crores allocated for the scheme.
Two-Phase Implementation: First phase focuses on strengthening existing facilities, second phase adds new equipment and services.
Coordination System: Multi-tier coordination cells from state to facility level for effective implementation.
Conditions for Availment
Services available only at government healthcare institutions.
Testing must be prescribed by qualified medical officers.
Regular maintenance and quality checks of equipment ensured through designated coordination cells.
eligibility:
The applicant must be seeking healthcare services at any government health facility in Gujarat.
The applicant must require diagnostic testing as prescribed by a qualified medical officer.
The applicant must visit designated government healthcare institutions including Sub Health Centers, Primary Health Centers, Community Health Centers, Sub-District Hospitals, District Hospitals, or Medical College hospitals.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: Visit Government Healthcare Facility
Visit the nearest government healthcare facility (Sub Health Center, Primary Health Center, Community Health Center, Sub-District Hospital, District Hospital, or Medical College hospital) during working hours with all required documents.
Step 2: Medical Consultation
Consult with the qualified medical officer or doctor available at the facility who will assess your medical condition and determine the necessary diagnostic tests required for proper diagnosis and treatment.
Step 3: Prescription Receipt
Obtain a written prescription from the medical officer specifying the required laboratory tests, X-ray, sonography, or Electrocardiography services needed for your medical condition.
Step 4: Laboratory Registration
Proceed to the laboratory or diagnostic section of the healthcare facility with your prescription and identity documents, where the laboratory technician will register your case and assign a unique identification number.
Step 5: Sample Collection and Testing
Undergo the prescribed diagnostic procedures including sample collection for laboratory tests, X-ray imaging, sonography, or Electrocardiography as per the medical officer's prescription under the supervision of trained technical staff.
Step 6: Report Collection
Collect your diagnostic test reports from the laboratory or diagnostic section as per the timeline specified by the facility, which may vary depending on the type of tests conducted.
Step 7: Follow-up Consultation
Return to the medical officer with your test reports for interpretation of results, further medical advice, and continuation of treatment as necessary for your healthcare needs.
documents_required:
Medical Prescription: From qualified government medical officer (original)
Patient Identity Proof: Any government-issued identity document (original and photocopy)
Medical History Records: Previous medical reports if available (original)
*Specific document requirements may vary based on the type of diagnostic test prescribed and the healthcare facility's protocols.

details: The "IELTS & TOEFL Training Assistance" scheme was launched by the Tribal Development Department, Government of Gujarat. It aims to empower Scheduled Tribe (ST) students aspiring to pursue higher studies abroad after class 12th. Under this scheme, the tribal students receive structured guidance, IELTS and TOEFL training, and financial assistance to prepare for admissions to reputed foreign universities, with additional support for visa and passport processes.

scheme_name: Vahan Aksmat Sahay Scheme
details: Launched in 2020, the scheme "Vahan Aksmat Sahay Scheme" by the Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide immediate medical assistance and financial support to victims of road traffic accidents to ensure timely treatment and reduce mortality rates. The scheme provides medical treatment packages ranging from ₹1,000/- to ₹45,000/- for various types of injuries and covers emergency medical procedures, surgeries, and hospitalization for accident victims. The applicant must be a victim of road traffic accident requiring immediate medical treatment and the treatment must be provided at empanelled hospitals under the scheme. The scheme is implemented by Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Gujarat and applications are accepted through participating hospitals where treatment is provided.
benefits:
Medical Treatment Packages
Comprehensive treatment packages ranging from ₹1,000/- to ₹45,000/- covering various types of injuries:
﻿

Package-wise Benefits
Simple Injury-I (Package 1): ₹1,000/- for treating simple wounds, injection Tetanus Vaccination, analgesic, antibiotics, general conditions check, cleaning, suturing and dressing
Simple Injury-II (Package 2): ₹2,500/- including face and scalp wounds, change of soiled cloth with disposable dress, intravenous line, multiple lacerated wounds suturing
Deglove Injury of Extremities (Package 3): ₹12,000/- for wound debridement in operation theatre under anaesthesia, intravenous line, intravenous antibiotics, remaining wounds suturing and inpatient treatment
Simple Fracture Without Anaesthesia (Package 4): ₹1,500/- for closed reduction, cleaning, suturing, dressing of wounds, fracture stabilization with splint
Closed Fracture Reduction Under Anaesthesia for First Fracture (Package 5): ₹4,000/- including closed fracture reduction under short general anaesthesia with plastering and inpatient treatment
Closed Fracture Reduction Under Anaesthesia for Additional Fracture (Package 6): ₹5,500/- for additional fractures with similar treatment as Package 5
Dislocation Reduction Under Anaesthesia (Package 7): ₹8,000/- for joint dislocation reduction under general anaesthesia and inpatient treatment
Open Reduction Internal Fixation-Small Bone (Package 8): ₹17,250/- including internal/external fixation of fracture in compound fractures, flap cover and inpatient treatment
Open Reduction Internal Fixation-Large Bone (Package 9): ₹40,250/- for large bone fractures with similar treatment as Package 8
Inter Costal Drainage One Side (Package 10): ₹8,000/- including intercostal drainage and inpatient treatment
Inter Costal Drainage Bilateral (Package 11): ₹11,000/- for bilateral intercostal drainage with inpatient treatment
Inter Costal Drainage Bilateral with Intensive Care Unit (Package 12): ₹12,000/- with Intensive Care Unit stay
Inter Costal Drainage Bilateral with Intensive Care Unit and Ventilator (Package 13): ₹14,000/- including ventilator support
Blunt Injury Abdomen (Package 14): ₹10,000/- for suspected blunt injury abdomen with stabilization as inpatient
Blunt Injury Abdomen with Intensive Care Unit Stay (Package 15): ₹12,000/- including Intensive Care Unit stay
Supra Pubic Cystostomy (Package 16): ₹5,000/- including supra pubic cystectomy and inpatient treatment
Immobilization for Suspected Spine Injury (Package 17): ₹5,000/- for non-operative management using spinal brace/collar
Cervical Spine Injury-Skeletal Tong Traction (Package 17): ₹25,000/- with tong traction
Spine Injuries with Computed Tomography/Magnetic Resonance Imaging (Package 18): ₹7,000/- including Computed Tomography scan/Magnetic Resonance Imaging
Tracheostomy (Package 19): ₹5,000/- for tracheostomy procedure
Head Injury with Intensive Care Unit Stay Conservative (Package 20): ₹7,000/- for non-operative management with Intensive Care Unit stay
Head Injury with Intensive Care Unit Stay with Ventilator (Package 21): ₹15,000/- including ventilator support
Head Injury with Intensive Care Unit Surgical Intervention (Package 22): ₹25,000/- for operative management including burr hole surgery
Blood Transfusion (Package 23): ₹1,500/- per unit (maximum admissible 3 units)
Road Traffic Accident Injuries Associated with Burns Conservative Management (Package 24): ₹4,000/- for conservative management of burns
Intensive Care Unit Stay Other than Head Injury (Package 25): ₹4,000/- for non-head injury Intensive Care Unit care
Pelvic Injury-Conservative Management (Package 26): ₹20,000/- for non-operative management with pelvic belt and traction
Pelvic Injury-External Fixation with Implant (Package 27): ₹45,000/- for stabilization with external fixation and haemodynamic stabilization
﻿

Mode of Disbursement
Cashless Treatment: Treatment provided at empanelled hospitals without upfront payment.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a victim of road traffic accident.
The applicant must require immediate medical treatment for injuries sustained in the accident.
The applicant must receive treatment at hospitals empanelled under the Vahan Aksmat Sahay Scheme.
The applicant must have Medico Legal Case registered for the accident.
The applicant must have police intimation with acknowledgment for the road traffic accident.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: Emergency Treatment at Empanelled Hospital
In case of a road traffic accident, the victim should be immediately taken to the nearest empanelled hospital under the Vahan Aksmat Sahay Scheme for emergency medical treatment. The hospital will provide immediate medical care as per the severity of injuries.
Step 2: Medico Legal Case Registration
The hospital authorities will ensure that a Medico Legal Case is registered with the local police station as it is mandatory for all road traffic accident cases. The hospital will coordinate with the police for proper documentation of the accident and injuries.
Step 3: Treatment Package Assessment
The treating doctor will assess the nature and severity of injuries and determine the appropriate treatment package from the 27 available packages under the scheme. The treatment will be provided as per the standardized protocols defined for each package.
Step 4: Documentation Collection
The hospital staff will collect all required documents including Medico Legal Case register details, police intimation copy, pre and post-treatment photographs, medical records, investigation reports, and hospital bills as specified for the respective treatment package.
Step 5: Cashless Treatment Provision
The empanelled hospital will provide cashless treatment to the accident victim as per the approved package amount. No upfront payment is required from the patient or family members for the covered treatment under the scheme.
Step 6: Claim Processing and Settlement
The hospital will submit the claim along with all required documents to the scheme authorities for processing and settlement. The payment will be directly settled between the government and the empanelled hospital.
Step 7: Discharge and Follow-up Care
Upon completion of treatment, the patient will be discharged with proper discharge summary and follow-up instructions. Patients are advised to retain copies of all medical records and treatment documents for future reference and any follow-up care requirements.
documents_required:
At Time of Treatment
Scanned Copy of Medico Legal Case Register Showing the Details of Injury
Police Intimation Copy with Police Acknowledgment
Two Photos Showing the Wound Before and After Treatment with Patient Face Clearly Along with the Treating Doctor (Includes Patient Photo for Identity)
Out-Patient Record/Case Sheet Details Showing the Treatment Given (Including Operation Theatre Notes If Any)
Investigation Done Report
Hospital Bill
 
Package-Specific Additional Documents
For Fracture Cases: X-Rays To Rule Out Fracture, X-Ray Showing the Fracture Before and After Stabilization
For Chest Injuries: Chest X-Ray Before and After Intercostal Drainage
For Abdominal Injuries: Blood Investigation, Chest X-Ray and X-Ray of Erect Abdomen, Ultrasound, Computed Tomography Scan Report (If Required), Electrocardiogram with Report (If Required)
For Spine Injuries: X-Ray Spine Anterior Posterior/Lateral, Computed Tomography/Magnetic Resonance Imaging
For Head Injuries: Computed Tomography Scan Report, Other Relevant Investigations

scheme_name: SAKSHAM - KVK 2.0
details: The scheme "SAKSHAM - KVK 2.0"was launched by the Gujarat Skill Development Mission (GSDM), Labour and Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide access to skill development programmes to a wide range of beneficiaries at taluka/village level, enhancing employability and competitiveness in the job market while promoting entrepreneurship. The scheme provides Short-Term Training in National Skills Qualifications Framework Level 1, 2, and 3 courses along with Gujarat Council of Vocational Training courses, and implements Special Projects for marginalized and vulnerable groups. The applicant must be a domicile of Gujarat, looking for fresh skills, reskilling, upskilling opportunities. The scheme is implemented by GSDM through empanelled Implementation Partners across all 33 districts of Gujarat. The applications are accepted online through the GSDM portal.
benefits:
Training Benefits
Short-Term Training of approximately 300 hours duration in National Skills Qualifications Framework/Gujarat Council of Vocational Training aligned courses.
On-the-Job Training as an integral part of Short-Term Training with duration determined by specific job roles.
Specialized training courses for Special Projects tailored to meet specific needs of marginalized and vulnerable groups.
Training conducted at Saksham Centers established at taluka/village level.
Access to certified Training of Trainers trainers endorsed by respective Sector Skill Councils.
Industry expert and entrepreneur guest lectures on rotational basis.
Assessment and Certification Benefits
Assessment and certification carried out by Sector Skill Councils/Gujarat Council of Vocational Training.
Assessment and certification fees paid directly by Gujarat Skill Development Mission.
Candidates eligible for assessment with minimum 70% attendance for classroom training and 70% for On-the-Job Training.
Employment Support Benefits
Placement assistance and employment opportunities from Implementation Partners.
Multiple placement options including wage employment, probation with stipend/salary, service aggregator portal registration, job work acceptance, self-employment, and apprenticeship opportunities.
Post-placement tracking and support services.
Infrastructure and Facility Benefits
Training centers with basic amenities including drinking water and separate toilets for boys and girls.
Aadhaar-based biometric attendance system and Internet Protocol-based Closed-Circuit Television cameras with Digital Video Recorder systems.
Teaching aids, furniture, fittings, and equipment as per job role requirements.
Conditions
Minimum 70% attendance required for classroom training to be eligible for completion.
Minimum 70% attendance required for On-the-Job Training to be eligible for completion.
Original Aadhaar card verification required during assessment to prevent duplication.
Adherence to batch timings from earliest 7:00 AM to latest 7:00 PM with minimum 4 hours and maximum 8 hours per day training.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a domicile of Gujarat.
The applicant must be minimum 14 years of age as per labour law for non-hazardous occupations.
The applicant must be maximum 59 years of age for Short Term Training.
The applicant must be maximum 65 years of age for Special Projects.
The applicant must be looking for fresh skills, reskilling, or upskilling opportunities for Short Term Training.
The applicant must be out of education, school/college dropout, or unemployed youth for Short Term Training.
The applicant must belong to marginalized, vulnerable groups requiring special attention or specific job roles for Special Projects.
Eligibility for Special Projects
The applicant must belong to one of the following categories: Jail Inmates, Former Prisoners, Juvenile homes, Scheduled Castes, Women, Members of the Transgender Community, Members of the Beggar Community, Senior Citizens and Old age homes, Patients recovered from Mental illness, Children in Shelter Homes and Orphanages, Scheduled Tribes, Economically Backward Communities, Persons with Disabilities, Rural Artisans, or Migrant Workers.
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Registration, Login and Profile Setup
Visit the official website of Gujarat Skill Development Mission and navigate to the SAKSHAM - KVK 2.0 application portal.
Create a new user account on the portal by providing basic personal information including name, mobile number, and email address. Verify your mobile number through OTP sent via SMS and verify email address through verification link.
Login to your registered account using credentials and complete your detailed profile including personal information, educational qualifications, employment status, and contact details. Upload a recent passport-size photograph in JPEG format with maximum file size of 100kb.
Step 2: Selection of Scheme, Job Role, and Training Center
Navigate to the SAKSHAM - KVK 2.0 scheme section and select the appropriate training category - either Short Term Training or Special Projects based on your eligibility and requirements. Browse through the available NSQF/GCVT aligned job roles listed on the portal. 
Select your preferred job role based on your interest, aptitude, and career objectives from the available options in your district/taluka.
View the list of empanelled Implementation Partners and their Saksham Centers in your preferred taluka/village location. 
Select your preferred training center based on location convenience and course availability.
Step 3: Filling Application Form and Document Upload
Fill the comprehensive application form with mandatory fields including personal details, educational background, employment history, family information, and preference details. Ensure all information is accurate and matches with supporting documents.
Upload all required documents in PDF format with maximum file size of 2MB per document. Ensure documents are clear, legible, and properly scanned with all four corners visible.
Step 4: Application Review and Submission
Review all entered information and uploaded documents for accuracy and completeness. Make necessary corrections if required and submit the application form. Note down the application reference number for future tracking.
Receive application submission confirmation message on registered mobile number and email address. Download and save the application acknowledgment receipt for future reference.
﻿

Grievance Redressal/Help and Support
For any queries or grievances, contact Gujarat Skill Development Mission at:
Address: Dr. Jivraj Mehta Bhavan, Block No. 2, Third Floor, Gandhinagar-382010
Contact Number: 079-232-53815

scheme_name: PMMSY: Establishment of open Sea cages (100-120)m3 vol. - Gujarat
details:
The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)” with the component of "Establishment of open Sea cages (100-120)m3 vol.", introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to modernise cold chain infrastructure to preserve fish health, uplift fishermen, and enhance value chain efficiency. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to fishermen and related beneficiaries for constructing ice plants or storage units with a minimum capacity of 30 tonnes, ensuring hygienic fish storage and better returns. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from the State Government.
﻿

Objective of the scheme
Marine production in the Gujarat state is one of the leading in the country. The state lags behind in exports due to non-norms. So cold storage/ice plant modernization is necessary to keep the fish in healthy condition.
Insulated vehicle, three wheeler with ice box including e-rickshaw, and live fish vending center to deliver the fish in hygienic condition to the consumer, so that the consumer (customer) can get fresh and clean fish from the local area and get good returns to the sellers.
Around 27,000 small and large boats are operating in the state. To strengthen the safety and security of the fishermen and increase the income of the boat owner, replacement of the existing boats is necessary to increase the sea growth of the existing boats.
benefits:
Establishment of Open Sea Cages (100-120 m³ volume):
The estimated unit cost is ₹5,00,000/- for the general category against ₹(Rs.) 40% assistance, i.e., ₹2,00,000/- or 40% of the purchase (whichever is less). 
60% contribution of the central government and 40% of the state government will be in the assistance received.
1 Sea-Cage unit under this scheme against the estimated unit cost of ₹ 5,00,00,000/- for women/SC/ST. 60% assistance for the category, i.e. assistance of ₹ 3,00,000/- or 60 % of the actual purchase, whichever is less, is available to the beneficiary.
60% contribution of the central government and 40% of the state government will be in the assistance received. The benefit will be available as per the target (category) allocated by the Government of India.
﻿

Remarks: 60% contribution from Government of India and 40% contribution from State Governments is paid.
Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits: Once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state.
The applicant should have a fishing boat registered under Real Craft software.
The applicant should hold a fishing license.
The applicant should complete the online registration on the iKhedut Portal.
The applicant should obtain in-principle approval before purchase of the floor plan.
The applicant should have his/her own bank account.
﻿

Preference/Reservation/Relaxation:
Women, SC, and ST beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost.
General category beneficiaries are eligible for 40% assistance of the unit cost.

Online

scheme_name: Vidhyadeep Yojana
details: The scheme “Vidhyadeep Yojana” was launched by the Education Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide insurance cover to students studying in primary, secondary, and higher secondary schools in the State. The scheme provides maximum financial assistance up to ₹50,000/- per student in case of accidental death or permanent total disability. The scheme is implemented by the State Insurance Fund (Directorate of Insurance) or private/public sector general insurance companies. The applications for this scheme are accepted by the Office of the District Primary Education Officer / District Education Officer / Administrative Officer (through the Principal of the school concerned).
benefits:
Accidental Death
₹50,000/- per student.
Accidental Permanent Disability
In case of loss of two eyes or both limbs or arm/leg due to accident: ₹50,000/-.
In case of loss of one eye and one limb due to accident: ₹50,000/-.
In case of loss of one eye or one limb due to accident: ₹25,000/-.
Conditions
The incident must be unexpected, sudden, and unintentional, and show visible signs of external violence.
The claim application must be submitted to the Nodal Officer within ninety (90) days from the date of the accident or death.
The benefit amount must not be paid for more than one category if the beneficiary is covered under multiple categories of the overall scheme.
eligibility:
For the Student Beneficiary
The beneficiary must be a student studying in all Primary, Secondary, and Higher Secondary schools of Gujarat.
For the Applicant/Claimant
The applicant must be the successor or the disabled beneficiary themselves in case of permanent disability.
The applicant must appear in person with the original identity card.
The applicant must submit the written application in the prescribed format to the local Nodal/Competent Officer.
The application must be submitted within ninety (90) days from the date of death or accident.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The applicant must obtain the necessary application forms and annexures through the Principal of the concerned school where the student was studying.
Step 2: The applicant must fully and comprehensively fill out the prescribed application form and gather all mandatory supporting documents to substantiate the claim. The application must be accompanied by the original Identity Card.
Step 3: The applicant (the heir in case of death, or the beneficiary in case of permanent disability) must submit the completed application along with all required proofs to the local Nodal/Competent Officer of the controlling office of the relevant department. This submission must be done in person (not via post) within ninety (90) days from the date of the accident or death.
Step 4: The Nodal Officer is responsible for fully checking and verifying the application and must forward the application, along with a certification in the prescribed format, to the Office of the Director of Insurance or the Insurance Company. This forwarding must occur within thirty (30) days of the Nodal Officer receiving the application.
﻿

Post-Application Processes
Step 1: Resolution by Insurer
The Insurance Company or the State Insurance Fund must decide on the claim approval or rejection within sixty (60) days of receiving the application with complete and satisfactory information/documents.
Step 2: Claim Closure Timeline
If the applicant fails to provide all requested documentation and fulfillments within the stipulated time, the claim will be closed automatically. The claim will also be permanently closed twelve months after the date of the accident/death, and no reconsideration request will be accepted thereafter.
﻿

*If a claim is rejected, the beneficiary or their heir must first submit a representation to the designated Review Committee within thirty (30) days.
*Legal action against the Review Committee's decision can only be pursued after filing the review, and the legal/quasi-judicial/judicial jurisdiction is exclusively in Gandhinagar.
*Claim inquiries or representations must be made only by the applicant or beneficiary themselves, excluding any third parties like agents or lawyers.
