scheme_name: S.T. Bus Pass Fee Concession Scheme For Government/ Non Government Primary, Secondary & Higher Secondary and Higher Technical Education Students
details: The scheme "S.T. Bus Pass Fee Concession Scheme For Government/ Non Government Primary, Secondary & Higher Secondary and Higher Technical Education Students" was launched by the Education Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide concession in bus fare fees to the students studying in Primary, Secondary, Higher Secondary, and Higher Secondary Technical Education in Government and Non-Government schools. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat State Road Transport Corporation (GSRTC). The application for this scheme is accepted online.
benefits: Concession in Fees of the Buses of Gujarat State Road Transport Corporation (GSRTC).
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a Student.
The applicant must be a Girl.
The applicant must be studying in Primary / Secondary / Higher Secondary / Higher Secondary Technical Education.
The applicant must be studying in a Government or Non-Government School.
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Visit the Official GSRTC E-Pass System website at https://pass.gsrtc.in. Alternatively, use the GSRTC mobile app (available on the Google Play Store).
Step 2: From the list of "Pass Type" categories, select "Student Pass". On the next page, click "New Pass Request". 
Step 3: The online application form will open. In the form, fill in the following mandatory fields:
Personal Details
Full name, complete postal address, date of birth, and gender.
Category details (General, SC, ST, SCBC) and educational qualification level.
Mobile number and email address for communication.
Organization/Institution Information
Name and address of the educational institution or workplace related to the pass application.
Travel Route and Service Details
Journey details including starting and destination points with route code. Add any via places and select the bus pass issue location.
Class of bus service required options include Express, AC Seater, AC Sleeper, City Service, Volvo, Luxury, etc.
Duration and number of days for the pass validity.
Step 4: Upload the following mandatory attachments in the specified format and size.
A recent passport-size photo (less than 10 KB in size).
Step 5: The form displays fees for bus pass, ID card amount if applicable, and any toll charges. The student concession will be applied automatically. Make the payment via UPI or counter payment at bus depots.
Step 6: Check the box agreeing to GSRTC's terms and conditions. Authenticate application submission by entering OTP sent to the registered mobile number. Submit the completed application form. An application number will be received for future tracking and status updates.
﻿

Important Information
Valid identity proof and enrollment certification from the educational institute may be required during verification.
Students are eligible for up to approximately 82.5% fare concession on the pass fare.
The pass is valid only on GSRTC buses as per the specified routes and class.
The online system supports registration, new applications, renewals, and application status inquiries.
Video tutorials and guides in Gujarati are available online to assist applicants throughout the process.
The GSRTC app facilitates easy access to pass details, bus schedules, and related services.

scheme_name: To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas: Fish Cage Culture - Gujarat
details:
The scheme “Fish Cage Culture” is a component under the “To Increase Fish Seed Production and Inland Fisheries Resources in Tribal Areas” scheme, introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and generate employment opportunities for individuals engaged in inland fisheries. Under this scheme, financial assistance is provided for activities such as fish cage culture and fish seed stocking, benefiting primarily the Scheduled Tribe (ST) communities involved in inland fisheries.
Objectives:
To increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities.
To boost fish farming or fishing activities in freshwater bodies.
Fishing in reservoirs, aquaculture in ponds, and catch from rivers increase fish production and boost inland fisheries.
The main objective is to provide employment opportunities to Scheduled Tribe fishermen in the state and increase fish production.
benefits:
Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost of ₹3,00,000/- to ₹1,80,000/-, or 60% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
Minimum Time Limit for Reclaiming Benefits: 3 year from the date of availing benefit.
eligibility:

scheme_name: Providing Latest Technology to Group of Pottery Artisans (Energy Efficient Kiln)
details: The scheme "Providing Latest Technology to Group of Pottery Artisans (Energy Efficient Kiln)" by the  Gujarat Matikam Kalakari & Rural Technology Institute, Industries & Mines Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide latest technology and equipment to pottery artisans for better productivity, improved quality and reduce health risks. The scheme provides 100% financial assistance on development of the kiln with unit cost of ₹83,000/- and energy-efficient furnaces to groups of registered clay artisans and potters. The applicant must be directly involved in the pottery business and be in the age group of 18 to 58 years, with at least 800 to 1000 square feet of open space available for pottery business. The scheme is implemented by the relevant Gujarat State Government Department and applications are accepted through prescribed application forms with required details and certificates.
benefits:
Financial Assistance: 100% financial assistance is provided on development of the kiln with unit cost of ₹83,000/-.
Technology Provision: Energy-efficient furnaces are provided to improve productivity and quality.
Health Benefits: Reduced health risks for pottery artisans through improved technology.
﻿

Mode and Frequency of Disbursement
The assistance will be given only in the form of an energy saving furnace.
No assistance related to the kiln will be paid in cash.
﻿

Conditions for Availment/Continuation
The energy-saving furnace must be used only for pottery business.
Beneficiaries must send a report about the utility of the furnace to the organization every 3 months after the furnace is ready.
Cost of maintenance and repair of the furnace must be borne by the beneficiaries at their own expense after construction and installation.
Misuse of the furnace will result in legal action by the institute.
eligibility:
The applicant must be registered clay artisans and potters eligible for the scheme on 100% subsidy basis.
The applicant must be directly involved in the pottery business and carry out pottery activity.
The applicant must be in the age group of 18 to 58 years.
The applicant must be part of a group doing at least 2 (two) pottery.
The applicant must have at least 800 to 1000 square feet of open space in their own rent lease or for at least five years on the date on which they apply for pottery business.
The applicant must be associated with pottery in the state of Gujarat only.
﻿

Post-Selection Conditions
The applicant must adhere to the policy rules of the institute as well as the norms regarding the construction of the kiln.
The applicant must use the energy-saving furnace only for pottery business.
The applicant must compulsorily use the energy-saving furnace as per the guarantee letter given unanimously.
application_process:
Online
Steps for New Registration
Step 1: Visit the eKutir portal. In the right pane of the landing page, click "For New Individual Registration, click Here".
Step 2: On the registration page, fill in all mandatory fields (marked with an asterisk): Full Name (in English), Aadhaar Card Number, Gender, Date of Birth, Mobile Number, E-mail, Password (confirm the password by typing it again). Fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Register".
Step 3: A confirmation pop-up will display the entered name, mobile number, and other details. If all the details are correct, click "Confirm" to proceed. If any information is incorrect, click "Cancel", make the necessary corrections, and then click "Register" again.
Step 4: Upon successful registration, the User ID and Password will be sent to your registered mobile number via SMS. If an email address has been provided, the credentials will also be sent via email.
﻿

Steps for Login Procedure and User Profile Update
Step 1: Visit the eKutir portal. In the right pane of the landing page (in the "Login to Portal" section), provide your User ID, Password, and the Captcha Code. Then click "Login".
Step 2: Upon first login, the user must complete the "User Profile" by providing personal and organisational details. All fields marked with an asterisk are mandatory. Some fields will already be pre-filled based on the information provided during registration.
*Most of these details can later be updated from the “View Profile” section, except for the fields initially filled during registration.
﻿

Steps for Scheme Application
Step 1: After logging in and completing the profile, the system displays schemes relevant to the user on the homepage. Click on the name of the scheme that you intend to apply for. You will be redirected to the online application form of the scheme. The application form is divided into multiple tabs. 
Step 2: In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. For each uploaded document, the corresponding document number must be entered wherever required. Click the “Save & Next” button to proceed to the subsequent tabs. 
Step 3: Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections. Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any) by ticking the designated checkbox.
Step 4: Clicking “Save & Next” in the last tab of the application form generates an application number, which is displayed on the screen. The applicant must record this number for future reference. If the applicant wishes to print the application, they may click the “Print Application” button.
*If you do not wish to continue with the application at that point, you may click "Cancel".
﻿

Application Printing and Modification
If the user wishes to print the application, they may click “Print Application.” 
If the application has been returned for revision, a “Modify” button will appear. The user can then edit the application and resubmit it.
﻿

Connect with the District Industries Centre (DIC) of your district.
﻿

scheme_name: Swayam Saksham Yojana (GTKVN)
category: Banking,Financial Services and Insurance, Business & Entrepreneurship
details:
The "Swayam Saksham Yojana" is implemented by the Gujarat Thakor and Koli Vikas Nigam (GTKVN) under the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to promote economic self-reliance among professionally educated unemployed youth (both men and women) from the Thakor and Koli communities, aged between 18 and 35 years, who hold degrees in fields such as Pharmacy, Architecture, Software/Hardware, Automobile Engineering, Law, Chartered Accountancy, and others.
Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of low-interest loans is provided to eligible beneficiaries who have completed higher professional education and wish to become self-reliant by starting their own business in their respective fields. It specifically supports individuals trained in professional fields like medicine, law, engineering, accounting, hotel management, and even wrestling or sculpture, to use their skills for gainful self-employment.
benefits:
Loan Amount:
Up to ₹5,00,000/-.
Interest Rate:
5% per annum.
Contribution Split:
95% of the business/project cost is covered under the scheme as a loan.
5% contribution must be made by the beneficiary.
Loan Repayment Terms:
Repayable in 60 equal monthly installments (maximum 5 years).
The repayment period depends on the nature of the business plan.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should belong to the Thakor or Koli caste/community. 
The applicant should be between 18 and 35 years of age on the date of application.
The applicant should have technical/professional education or experience in the field of business/trade.
The applicant should be unemployed and looking to start a self-employment unit.
The applicant family’s annual income should not exceed ₹3,00,000/-.
The applicant should have completed professional education in areas such as Medical, Law, Architecture, Engineering, Chartered Accountancy, Hotel Management, Software/Hardware, Financial Consultancy, Sculpture or Wrestling (with training) etc.
Note:
Out of total loans disbursed, at least 50% will be allocated to families with income up to ₹1,50,000/-.
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Visit the official website of the Gujarat Thakor and Koli Vikas Nigam.
Step 2: On the top menu bar, click on "Apply Now".

Step 4: Select the loan category and fill in the loan amount.
Step 5: Fill out the Application Form, enter the following details:
Personal Information: Name, gender, date of birth, caste, etc.
Address Information: Permanent and correspondence address
Income Details: Monthly family income and source
Loan Requirement: Amount needed, proposed use (e.g., business, self-employment)
Bank Account Details: For loan disbursement
Step 6: Upload scanned copies of the Caste certificate, Income certificate, Aadhaar card, Passport-size photo, Bank passbook (first page), and Residence proof.
Step 7: Double-check all details. Click on “Preview” to verify. If all details are correct, click “Submit”.
Step 8: Applicant will receive an Application ID/Reference Number. Save or print the acknowledgement for future tracking.

scheme_name: Bankable Scheme
category: Business & Entrepreneurship, Banking,Financial Services and Insurance
details: The "Bankable Scheme" is implemented by the Gujarat Scheduled Caste Development Corporation (GSCDC) under the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to scheduled caste persons for self-employment opportunities. Under this scheme, loans are provided for business, industry, or service activities, along with a subsidy of 50% of the loan amount or a maximum of ₹50,000/-. For setting up or expanding self-employment ventures in areas like animal husbandry, tailoring, groceries, beauty parlors, carpentry, RCC centering, footwear, bamboo work, embroidery, and other small-scale trades.
benefits:
Loan assistance up to ₹2,00,000/- for businesses, industries, and service activities.
Maximum loan of ₹1,00,000/- for business and ₹1,00,000/- for the industry/service sector individually.
Subsidy of 50% of the loan amount or up to ₹50,000/-, whichever is lower.
eligibility:
The applicant must be from a scheduled caste.
The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
The applicant's age must be between 18 to 60 years.
The applicant must be unemployed.
The applicant must be capable of undertaking business activity.
The applicant must not be a defaulter of any financial institution.
The applicant's annual income must not exceed ₹1,50,000/- (if residing in urban area) and ₹1,20,000/- (if residing in rural area).

scheme_name: Loan For Foreign Study (E.B.C.)
category: Banking,Financial Services and Insurance, Education & Learning, Social welfare & Empowerment
details: The scheme "Loan for Foreign Study (E.B.C.)" was launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide students belonging to the Educationally Backward Class (E.B.C.) with financial assistance for higher education abroad. The scheme provides a maximum loan of ₹15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakh) for higher studies in foreign countries. The applications for this scheme are accepted online via the official website https://esamajkalyan.gujarat.gov.in/.
benefits:
Financial Assistance
A maximum Foreign Study Loan of ₹15,00,000/- is provided for higher education abroad.
The loan is offered at a simple annual interest rate of 4%.
The loan covers costs such as air tickets, registration fees, tuition fees, education fees, hostel fees, books, and other expenses associated with the first year of the course abroad.
Disbursement Conditions
The full loan amount of ₹15,00,000/- will be disbursed to the student in a single installment.
The money will be deposited into the student's bank account after the submission of the original registered mortgage deed.
Post-Selection Obligations
After the sanction order is received, the student's parent or guardian must obtain the prescribed mortgage deed form from the District Office.
The property presented by the student's parent/guardian or the guarantor must be registered as a mortgage in favor of the government.
The loan repayment must begin one year after the completion of the student's study.
The loan must be repaid in monthly or quarterly installments.
The principal loan amount must be fully repaid within a maximum period of 10 years.
The interest amount must be fully repaid within a maximum period of 2 years.
The student must ensure all required documentation is completed within the prescribed time limit.
eligibility:
The applicant must belong to the Educationally Backward Class (S.E.B.C.).
The applicant's family annual income must be less than ₹10,00,000/-.
The applicant must have secured a minimum of 60% or more marks in 12th standard.
The applicant must have secured a minimum of 50% marks if they belong to Notified Tribes/De-notified Tribes (NT/DNT), More Backward, or Extremely Backward categories.
The applicant must be pursuing higher education abroad.
The applicant must be pursuing a Diploma, Graduate, Post Graduate, Post Graduate Diploma, Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.), or higher-level courses in any field.
The course must be for a minimum duration of 1 (one) academic year or more, or at least two semesters.
The applicant must apply before traveling abroad or within six months of arriving abroad.
The applicant must submit one solvent guarantor.
application_process:

Step 3: You will be taken to the online application form of the scheme. In the form, provide all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make corrections, if any.

Two identical physical copies of the complete application set, along with attached enclosures of all uploaded documents, must be prepared. These two copies must then be deposited at the respective District Office (District Social Welfare Officer) applicable to the student.

scheme_name: AGR 2 (Farm Mechanization) Scheme Of Farmers Other Than SC/ST
details:
The scheme “AGR 2 (Farm Mechanization) Scheme of Farmers Other Than SC/ST” was started by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat. Under the scheme, financial assistance is given to the farmers for the purchase of agricultural equipment to increase production of agricultural crops.
          The state government has launched the scheme to make agricultural industries successful through the use of agricultural mechanization. The farmer can get good quality and more farm produce at low cost by maximizing and utilizing natural, unimplemented sources such as land, water, and atmosphere due to agricultural mechanization. Thus, in order to increase agricultural mechanization, the scheme is started the state except in 208 talukas of the state where the Centrally Sponsored "Submissions on Agricultural Mechanization" scheme has already been implemented. This scheme is 100% sponsored by the state government.
benefits:
1.	The beneficiary will get financial assistance for the purchase of agricultural equipment/components such as Power Tillers, Rice Transplanter, Self-Propelled Machinery, Tractor/ Power Tiller operated Implements /tools, Electric Motor operated, etc.
2.	Under the scheme, more financial assistance will be given to the Small/Marginal/Women Farmers compared to other farmers.
eligibility:
The beneficiary should be a permanent resident of Gujarat State.
Any farmer of Gujarat state other than SC/ST (in 208 talukas where the Submissions on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM) scheme is not implemented) is eligible under this scheme.
The beneficiary farmer must purchase from the authorized manufacturer of the included manufacturer in the panels prepared for the purpose of periodic disclosed prize discovery by the Agriculture office/department.

scheme_name: Subsidy Scheme For Establishment Of Goat Unit (10+1) For Scheduled Caste People
details:
The scheme “Subsidy Scheme for Establishment of Goat Unit (10+1) for Scheduled Caste People” was launched by the Dept. of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare & Co-operation, Government of Gujarat to uplift economically poor livestock breeders of Gujarat State. Under the scheme, each beneficiary should purchase 10 female goats and 1 male goat and the beneficiary has to contribute 50% of the unit cost for food, water, fodder, residence, and concentrate expenses.
Terms and conditions of the scheme:-
The beneficiary has to purchase a goat's unit within 90 days of pre-approval of the online application. If the beneficiary fails to establish the goat unit within the time limit or proves disabled then the approval order given to the beneficiary will be canceled and the approval order will be given to the beneficiary who is next on the waiting list.
The Beneficiary has to give his/her photograph with the goat unit.
In general circumstances, the unit should be run for 3 years.
Beneficiary has to note the record of milk production and has to give register and information during the visit of the department technical officer.
benefits:
1.	Under the scheme, each beneficiary should purchase 10 female goats and 1 male goat. 1 to 2 year old female goats and a 2 to 3 years old buck should be bought.
2.	Under this scheme, the subsidy will be given 50% of the unit cost of the scheme (Maximum ₹45,000/-) for food, water, fodder, residence, and concentrate expenses.
eligibility:
This scheme can be applicable only to the adult beneficiaries of the Scheduled Caste people.
The benefit has been provided to only one person per family.
The Beneficiary has to give his/her photograph with the goat unit.
The beneficiary should purchase 1 to 2 year old female goats and a 2 to 3 years old buck.
The beneficiary has to contribute 50% of the unit cost of the scheme for food, water, fodder, residence, and concentrates expenses.
In general circumstances, the unit should be run for 3 years.
Note 01: The beneficiary has to purchase a goat's unit within 90 days of pre-approval of the online application. If the beneficiary fails to establish the goat unit within the time limit or proves disabled then the approval order given to the beneficiary will be canceled and the approval order will be given to the beneficiary who is next on the waiting list.
Note 02: Beneficiary has to note the record of milk production and has to give register and information during the visit of the department technical officer.

scheme_name: Assistance Scheme for Occupational Diseases (GBOCWWB)
details: The “Assistance Scheme for Occupational Diseases” is implemented by the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board (GBOCWWB), Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. Under the scheme, financial assistance is given to registered construction workers for 15 types of occupational diseases and 23 types of serious injuries.
benefits:
Free medical treatment and monthly assistance of ₹1500/- shall be provided to the registered construction worker under normal circumstances in case of less than 90% disability.
Free medical treatment and monthly assistance of ₹3000/- shall be provided to the registered construction worker in case of 90 to 100% disability.
The above assistance shall be provided up to a maximum limit of ₹3,00,000/-.
Financial assistance is provided to the registered construction workers for 15 types of occupational diseases and 23 types of serious injuries.
Terms and Conditions:
Labourers falling under the category of construction workers as determined by the State Government from time to time, who should be registered with the Board as construction workers, shall be treated free of cost in a hospital approved by the State Government.
eligibility:
The construction workers registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
The registered worker who has any type of disability due to an occupational disease or any serious injuries can avail of the benefits under this scheme.

details: The “Mukhyamantri Bhagyalaxmi Bond Scheme” is implemented by the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board (GBOCWWB), Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. The construction workers are eligible to avail the benefits of the scheme in the event of the birth of a girl child.
The benefit of the scheme is given upto one girl child.
The application form should be made within 12 months from birth.

scheme_name: Shramyogi Cycle Subsidy Scheme- Gujarat Labour Welfare Board
details: The “Shramyogi Cycle Subsidy Scheme” is implemented by the Gujarat Labour Welfare Board, Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. The objective of the scheme is to provide a subsidy for the purchase of a bicycle to laborers working in the organized sector, enabling them to commute between their residence and workplace.
benefits: Under the scheme, laborers will receive assistance of ₹1,500/- as a subsidy towards the purchase of a bicycle.
eligibility:
Labourers (Shramyogi) should be employed for the last one year in a factory/institute in the state of Gujarat.
Labourers who have been regularly contributing to the Labour Welfare Fund will be eligible for this scheme.
Assistance will be given only on the newly purchased cycle.
Application has to be made within 6 (six) months from the date mentioned in the bicycle purchase bill.
This scheme can be availed only once during the employment period.
Note 01: The bicycle should have a valid bill of purchase. Bill should have the name of the beneficiary, the shopkeeper's GST number, cycle chassis number, chassis should not be less than 22 inches. In case of a bill with incomplete details, assistance amount will not be given.
Note 02: The final decision regarding assistance will be with the Welfare Commissioner in Ahmedabad, ensuring justice in the field.

scheme_name: Laptop Purchase Assistance Scheme- Gujarat Labour Welfare Board
details: The “Laptop Purchase Assistance Scheme” is implemented by the Gujarat Labour Welfare Board, Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. The objective of the scheme is to encourage the children of laborers who are enrolled in professional or design courses by providing assistance for purchasing laptops, thereby reducing the financial burden of education.
benefits: Under the scheme, 50% of the amount or ₹25,000/- whichever is less will be given to the student admitted in a professional or designing course, with consideration to the laptop price limit of ₹50,000/-.
eligibility:
Workers who are employed in a factory/institute within the state of Gujarat and have been contributing to the Labour Welfare Fund for at least one year at the local office are eligible to apply for benefits under this scheme for their children.
The benefit of the scheme will be given to children of workers who have passed the class 12th board exam and secured all over 70% or more.
The student should be admitted in any professional or design course.
The laptop should be purchased in the name of the student.
Students who have taken admission abroad are not eligible to benefit from this scheme.
In the year in which the board exam has been given, the application will have to be made within 6 (six) months after purchasing the laptop in the same year.

Step 09: Agree with the Rules & Regulations and submit the application form. A confirmation email with the application Number will be sent on the registered email ID.

scheme_name: Private Tuition Assistance to Scheduled Caste Students Studying in Standard 11th & 12th (General Stream)
details: The “Private Tuition Assistance to Scheduled Caste Students Studying in Standard 11th & 12th (General Stream)” is implemented by the Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare, Government of Gujarat. Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to Scheduled Caste (SC) students who are studying in Standard 11th and 12th (General Stream) and have obtained 75% or more marks in the 10th Standard for private coaching fees, as per merit prepared at state level up to 100 students.
benefits:
Assistance Details:
•	Standard 11th: ₹8,000/- per annum as a private tuition fees
•	Standard 12th: ₹4,000/- per annum as a private tuition fees
eligibility:
The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste of Gujarat.
The student should be studying in Standard 11th & 12th General Stream.
The student should have 75% or more marks in Standard 10th.
The annual family income of the student should be up to ₹4.50 lakhs.
Note: Assistance is given to the first 100 eligible students based on merit.
application_process:
Offline
Step 01: To avail the benefits of the scheme, the students need to visit the nearest Office of the District Deputy Director (SCW) or Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare Office, Gandhinagar.
Step 02: The application form may be collected from the concerned office.
Step 03: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and attach all the mandatory documents.
Step 04: Submit the application form along with the documents to the concerned office.

scheme_name: Fellowship Scheme for M.Phil & Ph.D for Scheduled Caste Students (Maharaja Sayajirao Gayakvad Scholarship)
details: The “Fellowship Scheme for M.Phil & Ph.D for Scheduled Caste Students” scheme is administered by the Director, Scheduled Caste Welfare, Government of Gujarat. Under this scheme, Scheduled Caste students pursuing M.Phil and Ph.D. programs at any recognized universities will receive a monthly fellowship.
benefits:
Scholarship rate per student:
For M.Phil: ₹2,500/Per Month (For 10 Months)
For PhD:  ₹10,000/Per Month (For 10 Months)
eligibility:
The student should belong to the Scheduled Caste of Gujarat.
The student should be enrolled in either an M.Phil or Ph.D. program.
The annual family income of the student should be up to ₹6,00,000/-.
This assistance is paid on the condition that the student submits a thesis.
application_process:
Online
Application Process:
Step 01: The applicant may visit the Digital Gujarat Portal: https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in/﻿
Step 02: On the home page, click on ‘Register’ and the applicant will be redirected to the registration form page.
Step 03: Enter your Mobile Number, Email, Set Password and then click on ‘Save’.
Step 04: After successful registration, the applicant can login through his/her User Name (Mobile No./Email ID) and Password.
Step 05: Now, click on ‘My Profile’ to update your profile and fill in all the mandatory information.
Step 06: After filling in profile details, click on ‘Update Profile’. The profile will be updated after entering the verification Code (OTP).
Step 07: Now under services, select ‘Scholarship Services’ and then click on ‘SC Scheme’.
Step 08: Select the Financial Year for which you want to apply and then click on ‘Change Financial Year’.
Step 09: Select the scheme, read the interaction carefully, and then click on ‘Continue to Service’.
Step 10: Fill in the application form completely and upload all the relevant documents.
Step 11: Now, click on ‘Save as Draft’ and agree with the terms & conditions, and submit the application form by clicking on ‘Confirm & Final Submit’. 
Instructions: 
Note 01: Photo uploading for all applicants is mandatory.
Note 02: Application will be considered complete only when all sections are completed.
Note 03: Only one application per applicant will be acceptable for M.phil, PhD Thesis Assistance. If any student submits more than one application either from single institute or from multiple institutions his/her all applications will be rejected by the competent authority and no request from any side will be entertained.
Note 04: In case of any wrong/misleading information provided in the application shall lead to rejection of the application by Department Authorities.
Note 05: All required documents must be carefully scanned as per the instructions given.
Note 06: Student has to fill his/her application carefully as there is no provision for institution change once the application submits successfully.
Note 07: The unreadable uploaded documents will allow competent authority to reject student application.
Note 08: Direct Individual submission of this application form will not be accepted in case of students studying inside the state.

scheme_name: Dr. P. G. Solanki Scheme for Loan/Assistance to Medical Graduate Doctors of Scheduled Caste
details: The scheme “Dr. P. G. Solanki Scheme for Loan/Assistance to Medical Graduate Doctors of Scheduled Caste” was launched by the Director Scheduled Caste Welfare, Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat. The scheme was launched in the year 1976. Under the scheme, loans/financial assistance is provided to the Scheduled Caste Medical Graduates possessing qualifications such as M.B.B.S, B.S.A.M, B.A.M.S, B.A.M (Ayurveda), B.D.S (Dental) Homeopathic Degree/ BHMS and DHMS in Diploma, and Homeopathic Degree/Diploma (BHMS and DHMS in Diploma) to start their independent profession.
benefits:
Loan/Assistance details under the scheme:
Subsidy/assistance of ₹25,000/-.
Loan upto ₹2,50,000/- at the rate of interest 4%.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a Medical Graduate Doctor possessing qualifications such as M.B.B.S, B.S.A.M, B.A.M.S, B.A.M (Ayurveda), B.D.S (Dental) Homeopathic Degree/ BHMS and DHMS in Diploma, and Homeopathic Degree/Diploma (BHMS and DHMS in Diploma).
The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
The benefit of this scheme will be given only to natives of Gujarat state.
The annual income limit should not exceed ₹6,00,000/- for rural areas and ₹6,00,000/- for urban areas.
The applicant should not be employed in a government hospital or any private organization.
The benefit of this scheme will be available to one person in the family only once. If the involvement of another person is found, all loan assistance and other expenses will be recovered from the applicant.
After sanctioning the loan to the applicant, repayment will be made in monthly installments of ₹5,000/- for 12 months.
The applicant will have to start the clinic in 3 months after receiving government assistance.
Homeopathic doctors must practice only in pure homeopathy.

scheme_name: Prosthetic Aid and Appliances for Person with Disabilities
details: The scheme "Prosthetic Aids and Appliances for Person with Disabilities" was launched by the Director Social Defence, Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat. Under this scheme, a Person with Disabilities with 40% or more disability is given relief in his/her disability as well as equipment for self-employment within the limit of ₹20,000/-. The scheme is fully funded by the state government.
benefits:
Under this scheme, the following equipment is given within a limit of ₹20,000/-.
Artificial limb, crutch or calipers, tricycle, folding wheelchair to Person with Disabilities.
Hearing aids and other tool assistance for mute and deaf persons.
Musical instruments, brail kit for visually impaired persons.
M. R. child kit for mentally challenged persons.
For self-employment, hand carts, sewing machines, instruments of electric repairing, tools of cycle repairing, hair cutting kit, etc.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a native of Gujarat.
The applicant should have 40 % or more disability.
Employment-oriented tools will not be provided to individuals under the age of 16.
Under this scheme, employment-oriented and disability-relieving equipment will be available only once in a period of 5 years.
Both employment-oriented and disability-relieving equipment are available for persons with disability under the scheme.

scheme_name: Assistance for Power Driven Chaff Cutter Scheme under Tribal Area Sub Plan (TASP- ST Category)
details: The scheme “Assistance for Power Driven Chaff Cutter Scheme under Tribal Area Sub Plan” is implemented by the Directorate of Animal Husbandry, Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat. Under this scheme, financial assistance is given to dairy farmers belonging to the Scheduled Tribes (ST) category on purchase of electric powered chaff cutters. By using power driven chaff cutter, dairy farmers can reduce wastage of green and dry fodder which is economically important for their profitable dairy farming.
benefits:
Under the scheme, financial assistance up to 75% of the purchase cost of powered chaff cutter or ₹18,000/-, whichever is less, is given to beneficiaries of Scheduled Tribes category.  
Note: The amount of assistance will be deposited in bank account of the beneficiary.
eligibility:
The beneficiary should be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
The beneficiary should belong to the Scheduled Tribe category.
The beneficiary should have a minimum of five (5) animals of his/her own.

Step 03: Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button and an application page will open.

Step 05: Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.

Step 07: Take a print out of the confirmed application.
Step 08: Applicant can also check their Application Status/View/Print/Upload Application details from the below link: https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/public/frm_Applicant_Corner.aspx ﻿
Note: After sanctioning application through I-Khedut Portal, beneficiary has to purchase Chaff Cutter from government approved empanelled vendor and has to produce the bill for claim submission of subsidy.

The scheme is fully funded by the state government.
benefits:

The benefit of free travel is also provided up to the final/last station of the bus route in the case of travel outside the state of Gujarat.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana
details: The "Mukhyamantri Matrushakti Yojana (MMY)" launched in 2022 by the Women and Child Development Department, Gujarat, focuses on improving the health and nutrition of pregnant women and mothers with children under 2 years. The scheme offers a nutritional kit containing groundnut oil, tuver dal, and chana to support calorie and protein needs. The initiative addresses the critical first 1000 days of life, aiming to enhance maternal and child health, reduce infant mortality, and prevent low birth weight.
benefits: Commodities: The nutrition kit includes 1 Litre of Groundnut oil, 1 Kg Tuver Dal, & 2 Kg Chana.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a woman who is conceiving for the first time.
The applicant must be a mother with her first child who is under the age of 2 years.

If the applicant is a girl studying in Std. 9 to 12, she must be staying in a hostel run by a society, trust, or institution.

scheme_name: Development of Brackish Water Aquaculture
details: The scheme "Development of Brackish Water Aquaculture" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to enhance the income of brackish water fishermen by promoting modern shrimp farming techniques, improving disease control methods, increasing employment, and boosting shrimp exports. The scheme provides financial assistance in the form of subsidies for shrimp farm development, equipment purchase, and related activities.
benefits:
Name of Component	Standard of Assistance	Remarks	Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits
Subsidy on purchase of  Aerator	50% subsidy on unit cost of Rs.50, 000/- or 50% of  the actual cost incurred whichever is less will have to be sanctioned.		Subsidy is given to Shrimp Farm owner		Once in 3 years
Subsidy on Construction of New shrimp farm		50% subsidy on unit cost of Rs.7.00 lakh per Hector or 50% of the actual cost incurred whichever is less will have to be sanctioned.		Subsidy is given to land owner or beneficiary should have land on lease for 10 years. If govt. land is allotted by collector then land allotment order must be enclosed with  applicatio	Once in lifetime
Subsidy on Renovation of shrimp farm		Subsidy on unit cost of Rs.20, 000/- Per hector or 50% of the actual cost incurred whichever is less will have to be sanctioned.		Subsidy is given to Shrimp Farm owner		Once in 3 years
Subsidy on Bird Fencing / Dog Fencing	For bird fencing 50% subsidy of unit cost of Rs. 10,000/- per hector. For Dog fencing 50% subsidy of unit cost of Rs. 25,000/- per hector.	Subsidy is given to Shrimp Farm owner		Once in 3 years
Subsidy on purchase of  shrimp feed and seed		50% subsidy on unit cost of Rs.3.00 lakh per Hector or 50% of the actual cost incurred whichever is less will have to be sanctioned		Subsidy is given to Shrimp Farm owner		Once in 3 years
Sea weed culture	For Tube net:- 50% subsidy on unit cost of Rs.8000/-  or 50% of the actual cost incurred whichever is less will have to be sanctioned. For Raft:- 50% subsidy on unit cost of Rs.3000/-  or 50% of the actual cost incurred whichever is less will have to be sanctioned.	Subsidy on purchasing of tube net/Raft is given for culture of the sea weed.		Once in 3 years
construction of Effluent Treatment System (ETS)	50% of unit cost of Rs. 11,00,000/- per hector or 50% of the actual cost incurred whichever is less will have to be sanctioned	Subsidy is given to Shrimp Farm owner		Once in lifetime
Shrimp Hatchery	50% of unit cost of Rs. 400.00 lakh or 50% of the actual cost incurred whichever is less will have to be sanctioned		Subsidy is given to land owner or beneficiary should have land on lease for 10 years. The production capacity of the shrimp hatchery must be 100 million P.L/ year.	Once in lifetime
eligibility:
The applicant must have own land or government-allotted land for shrimp farming.
Shrimp Farm of the Beneficiary should have been registered with coastal aquaculture authority or beneficiary should have been applied for it.
The beneficiary must have his own bank account.
The applicant must provide valid identity proof (Voter ID, Aadhaar Card, Ration Card, or Passport).

scheme_name: Assistance of Concentrate Feed for Pregnant Animals (Scheduled Tribe Category)
details: The scheme "Assistance of Concentrate Feed for Pregnant Animals (Scheduled Caste Category)" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to support Scheduled Tribe livestock owners during the last trimester of cattle pregnancy—a critical period for both the animal and the calf. Through this scheme, 150 kg of BIS Type II concentrate feed is provided free of cost to eligible beneficiaries to enhance milk yield, extend the milking period, and prevent health complications.

The animal/ cattle should be in the last trimester of pregnancy.

scheme_name: Uniform Assistance for Other Backward Classes (Minority) Students Studying in Standard 1st to 8th
details: The scheme "Uniform Assistance for Other Backward Classes (Minority) Students Studying in Standard 1st to 8th" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat aims to promote education and reduce the economic burden on families of minority students from the Other Backward Classes (OBC) by providing them with uniform assistance. The scheme ensures that eligible students from classes 1 to 8 studying in government and aided primary schools receive ₹900/- to cover the cost of three sets of school uniforms.
benefits: Financial Assistance: ₹900/- is provided to each eligible student for the purchase of three pairs of school uniforms.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a student.
The applicant should belong to the state of Gujarat.
The applicant should be studying in Class 1st to Class 8th.
The applicant should be enrolled in a government or government-aided primary school.
The applicant must belong to the Other Backward Classes (OBC) under the minority category.

scheme_name: Stipends to Students of NT/DNT Caste for Technical and Vocational Courses after SSC
details: The scheme “Stipends to Students of NT/DNT Caste for Technical and Vocational Courses after SSC” by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat aims to promote inclusive education by financially supporting students from economically weaker sections pursuing technical and vocational education after SSC. The initiative offers a stipend of ₹400 per month to aid the academic progress and reduce the dropout rate among underprivileged youth. The scheme is implemented by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department. It specifically benefits those enrolled in professional or Government ITI courses and belonging to families with an annual income not exceeding ₹6,00,000/-.
benefits: Stipend: A monthly stipend of ₹400/- is given to NT/DNT Caste students for one year of recognized professional and Government ITI courses.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should belong to Nomadic Tribe and Denotified Tribe (NTDNT) communities.
The applicant should be a student who has passed the Secondary School Certificate (SSC) examination.
The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized professional or Government Industrial Training Institute (ITI) course.
The applicant’s individual and family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/- per annum.

scheme_name: Award of Fellowship to Most Eminent Artist
details: The scheme "Award of Fellowship to Most Eminent Artist" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to recognize and financially assist artists aged 60 years or above who have retired from professional artistic practice and have made distinguished contributions in the field of plastic arts at the national level. The fellowship is intended to support such artists by enabling them to acquire necessary equipment and continue contributing meaningfully to the arts. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat State Fine Arts Academy. In this scheme, 10 artists are given a monthly fellowship of ₹5,000/- for one year, and it is extendable for a maximum of two years, depending on the quality and impact of the artist’s continued work.
benefits: Monthly Fellowship: ₹5,000/-.
eligibility:
The applicant should be an artist.
The applicant must be 60 years or older.
The applicant should be retired from the professional field of plastic arts.
The applicant should have attained national-level fame and established a distinct identity through excellence in painting, sculpture, or graphic arts.

Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the supporting documents to the Gujarat State Fine Arts Academy.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority at the Academy office to whom the application has been submitted.

scheme_name: Publication Support Scheme for Fine Arts

details: The scheme "Ambubhai Purani Award" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to promote the development of gymnasiums and traditional Indian physical culture by honoring individuals who have shown exceptional contribution in this field. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹51,000/- and state-level recognition to outstanding individuals, fostering motivation and continuity in preserving indigenous physical training traditions. benefits: Financial Assistance: ₹51,000/-.

scheme_name: Manav Kalyan Yojana
details: Launched on 11th September 1995, the scheme "Manav Kalyan Yojana" implemented by the Commissioner of Cottage and Rural Industries, Industries & Mines Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to uplift economically disadvantaged sections by providing toolkit assistance (via e-vouchers) for self-employment in 10 designated trades. The scheme replaces previous self-employment initiatives and targets individuals/families with an annual income of up to ₹6,00,000/- in rural/urban areas. It focuses on empowering artisans and marginalized communities, including SC/ST and nomadic tribes, with no income limit for the most backward sub-castes. Applications are submitted online via the e-Kutir portal, and beneficiaries receive toolkits through a transparent e-voucher system.
benefits:
Toolkit Assistance: E-vouchers for toolkits in 10 specified trades.
Trades Covered: Dairy Product Sales, Plumber, Bharat Kaam (Embroidery), Centering Work, Beauty Parlor, Electric Appliances Repairing, Papad Making, Pickle Making, Vehicle Servicing, Puncture Kit
*The maximum cost limits per toolkit are predefined (exact amounts not specified in the document).
Training Option: Applicants can opt for skill training during the application process.
Transparent Disbursement: E-vouchers (QR codes) are generated post-approval and redeemed at approved dealers.
eligibility:
The applicants must be between 18 and 60 years of age to qualify for the scheme.
The applicants from Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), or Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) nomadic tribes are eligible without any income proof requirement.
The applicants from general categories must have a family annual income of ₹6,00,000/- or less, and must submit an income certificate as proof.
﻿

Additional Requirement for Rural Applicants
The rural applicants must - 
a) either be listed in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) list with a poverty score of 0 to 16.
b) or submit an income certificate issued by the relevant authority.
﻿

Income Criteria Exemptions
The income limit does not apply to the following:
a) Individuals from the 12 most backward sub-castes within the Scheduled Castes.
b) Members of the most backward, nomadic, or de-notified tribes under the SEBC category.
*For all other applicants, the annual family income must not exceed ₹6,00,000/-, whether residing in rural or urban areas.
application_process:
Online
Steps for New Registration
Step 1: Visit the eKutir portal. In the right pane of the landing page, click "For New Individual Registration, click Here".
Step 2: On the registration page, fill in all mandatory fields (marked with an asterisk): Full Name (in English), Aadhaar Card Number, Gender, Date of Birth, Mobile Number, E-mail, Password (confirm the password by typing it again). Fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Register".
Step 3: A confirmation pop-up will display the entered name, mobile number, and other details. If all the details are correct, click "Confirm" to proceed. If any information is incorrect, click "Cancel", make the necessary corrections, and then click "Register" again.
Step 4: Upon successful registration, the User ID and Password will be sent to your registered mobile number via SMS. If an email address has been provided, the credentials will also be sent via email.
﻿

Steps for Login Procedure and User Profile Update
Step 1: Visit the eKutir portal. In the right pane of the landing page (in the "Login to Portal" section), provide your User ID, Password, and the Captcha Code. Then click "Login".
Step 2: Upon first login, the user must complete the "User Profile" by providing personal and organisational details. All fields marked with an asterisk are mandatory. Some fields will already be pre-filled based on the information provided during registration.
*Most of these details can later be updated from the “View Profile” section, except for the fields initially filled during registration.
﻿

Steps for Scheme Application
Step 1: After logging in and completing the profile, the system displays schemes relevant to the user on the homepage. Click on the name of the scheme that you intend to apply for. You will be redirected to the online application form of the scheme. The application form is divided into multiple tabs. 
Step 2: In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. For each uploaded document, the corresponding document number must be entered wherever required. Click the “Save & Next” button to proceed to the subsequent tabs. 
Step 3: Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections. Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions, declaration, and privacy policy (if any) by ticking the designated checkbox.
Step 4: Clicking “Save & Next” in the last tab of the application form generates an application number, which is displayed on the screen. The applicant must record this number for future reference. If the applicant wishes to print the application, they may click the “Print Application” button.
*If you do not wish to continue with the application at that point, you may click "Cancel".
﻿

Application Printing and Modification
If the user wishes to print the application, they may click “Print Application.” 
If the application has been returned for revision, a “Modify” button will appear. The user can then edit the application and resubmit it.
﻿

Helpdesk/Grievance Redressal
Call 9909926280 / 9909926180.
Connect with the District Industries Centre (DIC) of your district.
﻿

Check Application Status
Visit the Application Status page Provide the Application Number of the scheme, and your Date of Birth. Then click "View Status".

scheme_name: Small Scale Lending Scheme (Microfinance)
category: Social welfare & Empowerment, Banking,Financial Services and Insurance
details: The scheme "Small Scale Lending Scheme (Microfinance)" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide microcredit to the small entrepreneurs of the target group. The scheme provides loans up to ₹1,25,000/- at subsidized interest rates of 5% per annum to eligible beneficiaries. To be eligible, the applicant must belong to the Rabari and Bharwad castes among the socially and educationally backward classes and must be aged between 21 to 45 years. The scheme is implemented by Gujarat Gopalak Vikas Nigam. The applications for this scheme are accepted online through the Department of Agriculture and Animal Husbandry (DAAD) Online portal.
benefits:
Loan Amount: Maximum loan limit of ₹1,25,000/-.
Interest Rate: Subsidized interest rate of 5% per annum.
Loan Coverage: 95% of the project cost is covered through a loan.
Beneficiary Contribution: Only 5% contribution is required from the beneficiary.
Target Group Support: Specifically designed for small entrepreneurs from the Rabari and Bharwad communities.
eligibility:
The applicant must belong to the Rabari and Bharwad castes among the socially and educationally backward classes.
The applicant must be aged between 21 to 45 years.
The applicant must have an annual income of less than or equal to ₹3,00,000/-.

scheme_name: Pandit Omkarnath Classical Music Festival and Awards
details: The scheme "Pandit Omkarnath Classical Music Festival and Awards" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to preserve, promote, and celebrate the Indian classical music heritage by recognizing and awarding distinguished male artists who have significantly contributed to this field. The award is conferred annually during the Pt. Omkarnath Shastri Sangeet Mohotsav, where artists are selected by an expert committee based on their lifelong service, adherence to the guru-shishya tradition, and national or international recognition in classical music.
benefits: Financial Assistance and Recognition: Eligible male artists receive a prestigious award that acknowledges their lifelong dedication to Indian classical music.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a male.

The applicant should have significantly contributed to the field of Indian classical music.
The applicant should have rendered at least 40 years of service in classical music at the state or national level.
The applicant should have received formal training under the traditional guru-shishya parampara (teacher-disciple tradition).
The applicant should have worked for the promotion, protection, and nurturing of classical music.
The applicant should have received recognized awards at the national or international level.
The applicant should have conducted noteworthy research in the development and conservation of classical music.

scheme_name: Girnar Arohan-Avrohan Competition
details: The scheme “Girnar Arohan-Avrohan Competition” by the Department of Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities, Government of Gujarat, aims to encourage physical endurance, sports participation, and cultural spirit among individuals by organizing an annual state-level stair-climbing competition on Mount Girnar. Out of the 9,900 steps of the mountain, the competition mandates male participants to complete 5,500 steps and female participants to complete 2,200 steps. Participants who secure positions among the top 10 are rewarded with substantial cash prizes, thereby motivating individuals of all backgrounds to embrace physical challenges and celebrate Gujarat’s heritage.
benefits: Cash Prize: A total of ₹8,40,000/- is distributed among the first 10 winners.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should be physically fit and willing to participate in the stair-climbing competition.
The applicant should complete the required number of steps: 5,500 steps for men and 2,200 steps for women.

scheme_name: International Financial Assistance
details: The scheme "International Financial Assistance" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat aims to encourage and support sportspersons from Gujarat participating in international-level competitions by providing a one-time financial assistance of ₹75,000/- to cover costs related to boarding, lodging, air travel, kit, or miscellaneous expenses. This initiative ensures that deserving athletes are not hindered by financial constraints in their pursuit of excellence on global platforms.
benefits: Financial Assistance: ₹75,000/- is provided to eligible sportspersons to cover expenses for boarding, lodging, travel (including air tickets), sports kits, and other related miscellaneous expenses.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a sportsperson representing Gujarat in any recognized international-level sports competition.
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should be male.

scheme_name: Trekking in Coastal Area for Scheduled Caste
details: The scheme "Trekking in Coastal Area for Scheduled Caste" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to offer Scheduled Caste youth a direct introduction to the culture of Sagarkantha, increase awareness about coastal industries and marine wealth, and motivate them to engage in ocean-related developmental activities.
benefits: Cultural and Industrial Exposure: The program provides 100 Scheduled Caste youth annually with a 10-day trekking tour to explore the culture of Sagarkantha, learn about coastal wealth, and understand local industries.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste category.
The applicant should be between 15 and 35 years of age.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The interested applicant should download the prescribed format of the application form.
Step 2: In the application form, provide all the mandatory details, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the supporting documents to the office of the District Youth Development Officer.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted.
documents_required: Aadhaar Card.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Small Biofloc Establishment Gujarat
details:
The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)" with a component of Small Biofloc Establishment (7 tanks 4mm diameter and 1.5m height) introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities.
Through this scheme, inland fish farming is promoted using advanced technologies such as Biofloc and Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) to maximise fish production in less land and water. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
﻿

Objective: 
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries. 
Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits:
Small Biofloc Establishment (7 tanks 4mm diameter and 1.5m height):
Financial Assistance:
The unit cost is ₹7,50,000/- per unit.
General Category: 40% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
Women/Scheduled Caste (S.C.) and Scheduled Tribe (S.T.) Categories: 60% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
﻿

Mode of Disbursement: Assistance is paid through 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from State Governments.
Validity: The benefit for small biofloc establishment can be reclaimed once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.
The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
﻿

Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

scheme_name: National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture - Reinforced Area Development
details: The scheme "National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture - Reinforced Area Development" is a centrally sponsored scheme that aims to promote location-specific sustainable agriculture production by encouraging improved agronomic practices, soil health management, better rainwater utilization, judicious use of agrochemicals, crop diversification, and the progressive adoption of integrated farming systems involving crops, livestock, and trees.
benefits:
Financial Help: Provides financial assistance to eligible farmers to adopt sustainable agricultural practices.
Improved Agronomic Practices: Encourages the implementation of location-specific, modern, and efficient farming techniques.
Soil Health Management: Supports initiatives for maintaining and enhancing soil fertility and health.
Enhanced Rainwater Use Efficiency: Promotes techniques and infrastructure for optimal utilization of rainwater in agriculture.
Judicious Use of Chemicals: Advocates for reduced and responsible application of chemical fertilizers and pesticides.
Crop Diversification: Encourages farmers to cultivate a variety of crops, promoting resilience and better income opportunities.
Integrated Farming Systems: Supports the adoption of integrated crop-livestock-tree farming for holistic farm management.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should be a farmer.

scheme_name: Scheme for Fruit and Vegetable Preservation and Training Program in Horticulture Department HRT 5
details: The "Scheme for Fruit and Vegetable Preservation and Training Program in Horticulture Department HRT 5" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to empower women and urban citizens by offering hands-on training in value addition, preservation, canning, and kitchen-gardening of fruits and vegetables, along with promoting self-sustenance and nutritional well-being. scheme_name: Assistance for Protected Cultivation
details: The scheme “Assistance for Protected Cultivation” by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat aims to promote modern, climate-resilient horticulture practices by providing financial support for protected cultivation infrastructure like mulching, nursery development, and plug nurseries. The scheme encourages self-employment and sustainable income generation for farmers through the use of polyhouses, shed net houses, and starter kits to improve crop productivity and resource efficiency.

Hilly Area: ₹36,800/- per hectare.

₹3,00,000/- per unit for 500 sq.m. area

scheme_name: Distribution of Seeds of more productions varieties/ Hybrids varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area: Agricultural Fair/Exhibition/Farmer Camp Gujarat
details: The scheme "Distribution of Seeds of more productive varieties / Hybrid varieties Seeds and fertilizer at subsidies etc. to Adivasi farmers in Tribal Area (TASP)" with a component "Agricultural Fair/Exhibition/Farmer Camp" is introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide subsidized agricultural inputs to enhance productivity and livelihood of tribal farmers. Farmers belonging to scheduled tribes and tribal cultivators residing outside tribal areas can achieve higher production of agricultural crops and improve their livelihood by using plant protection equipment, tarpaulins, field demonstrations, micronutrients, agriculture fairs, water carrying pipelines, castor cake/neem cake/LFOM/green manure, pump sets, 100% water-soluble fertilizer, plant protection pesticides, etc. These tools and practices, when used according to scientific recommendations, can significantly increase crop production and, consequently, improve their livelihood.
benefits:
The assistance is provided with a limit of ₹1,00,000/- can be spent for holding Agricultural Fair/Exhibition at the Taluka level. Farmer Shibir for 100 farmers, ₹15000/- can be spent within the limit.
﻿

eligibility:
The applicant should belong to a Scheduled Tribe.
The applicant should be a farmer.
The applicant should be located in tribal areas OR be a tribal cultivator residing outside tribal areas.
The scheme excludes farmers from Dang district.
application_process:
Offline
Approach the Gram Sevak in your village for access to the Agricultural Fair/Exhibition/Farmer Camp under the scheme.

scheme_name: Rotavator/Thresher Machine Equipment Assistance Scheme
details: The “Rotavator/Thresher Machine Equipment Assistance Scheme” was launched by the Tribal Development Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to strengthen agricultural mechanization and improve the productivity of Scheduled Tribe farmers in tribal areas. Under this scheme, tribal farmers are provided with financial assistance to purchase essential farm machinery such as a 6-feet rotavator or a thresher machine, which they cannot afford due to poor economic conditions.

The applicant should be a farmer dependent on agriculture as the main source of livelihood.
The applicant should intend to use the machinery for self-employment and agricultural purposes.

scheme_name: Swami Vivekananda Exam Oriented Residential Training
details: The scheme "Swami Vivekananda Exam Oriented Residential Training" was launched by the Directorate of Employment and Training, Department of Labour and Employment, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide comprehensive residential training to aspiring candidates for recruitment in Army, Air Force, Navy, Paramilitary Forces and Police Forces, addressing the need for specialized coaching and preparation to enhance success rates in defence and police recruitment examinations. The scheme provides exam-oriented residential training programs and coaching facilities specifically designed for defence services examinations and conducts comprehensive preparation sessions covering written examinations, physical fitness training, personality development, and interview preparation. The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat state and must be aspiring for recruitment in Army, Air Force, Navy, Paramilitary Forces or Police Forces with appropriate age and educational qualifications as per the respective service requirements. The scheme is implemented by the Directorate of Employment and Training under the Labour and Employment Department, Government of Gujarat and applications for this scheme are accepted through designated employment exchanges and training centers across the state.
benefits:
Training and Coaching Support
Residential Training: Comprehensive exam-oriented coaching in residential format.
Defence Examination Preparation: Specialized training for Army, Air Force, Navy recruitment examinations.
Police Force Training: Preparation for Police Force recruitment examinations.
Paramilitary Force Coaching: Training for Central Armed Police Forces and other paramilitary recruitment.
﻿

Training Components
Written Examination Preparation: Subject-wise coaching and mock tests.
Physical Fitness Training: Physical preparation as per service requirements.
Personality Development: Communication skills and confidence building.
Interview Preparation: Mock interviews and counseling sessions.
﻿

Conditions
Regular attendance in all training sessions and activities.
Compliance with residential training center rules and regulations.
Participation in mock tests and assessment programs.
Maintenance of discipline and conduct as per training center guidelines.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be aspiring for recruitment in Army, Air Force, Navy, Paramilitary Forces or Police Forces.
The applicant must meet the age criteria as prescribed for respective defence and police services.
The applicant must possess minimum educational qualifications as required for the specific service examination they are preparing for.
The applicant must have completed relevant educational requirements as per Central Government norms for defence services.
The applicant must meet basic physical fitness standards for defence and police services.
The applicant must be medically fit as per general health requirements.
The applicant must be willing to undergo rigorous physical training programs.
The applicant must belong to economically weaker sections or demonstrate financial need for training assistance.
The applicant must not be receiving similar training assistance from other government schemes simultaneously.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: Registration with Employment Exchange
The applicant must first register with the nearest District Employment Exchange office in Gujarat and obtain a valid employment registration number for accessing training programs.
Step 2: Medical and Physical Fitness Assessment
The applicant must undergo preliminary medical check-up and physical fitness assessment at the designated center to ensure they meet basic standards for defence training.
Step 3: Training Program Inquiry
The candidate should visit the Directorate of Employment and Training office / the nearest District Employment Office / the Designated Training Center and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form for the scheme "Swami Vivekananda Exam Oriented Residential Training".
Step 4: Form Completion and Documentation
The candidate should fill the application form completely with accurate personal details, educational background, preferred service category (Army/Navy/Air Force/Police/Paramilitary), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 5: Application Submission and Interview
The completed application with all documents must be submitted at one of the following - 
Directorate of Employment and Training, Block No. 1, 3rd Floor, Dr. Jivraj Mehta Bhavan, Gandhinagar - 382010.
District Employment Exchanges across all districts in Gujarat.
Designated Training Centers (as notified by the Directorate of Employment and Training).
*The applicant is advised to save a copy of the submitted application form and acknowledgment receipt for future reference.
Step 6: Selection and Admission
The candidate may be required to appear for a preliminary interview or counseling session. Based on the evaluation of application, documents verification, and interview performance, selected candidates will be notified about their admission to the residential training program.
Step 7: Training Commencement
Selected candidates must report to the designated training center on the specified date with necessary personal items and documents for the commencement of the residential training program.
documents_required:
Educational Certificates (10th, 12th and Higher Education as applicable)
Domicile Certificate Of Gujarat State
Income Certificate Of Family
Caste Certificate (If Applicable for Reservation)
Medical Fitness Certificate From Registered Medical Practitioner
Physical Fitness Certificate From Competent Authority
Aadhaar Card (Copy)
Birth Certificate Or Age Proof Document
Passport Size Photographs (Recent)
Bank Account Details (Passbook Copy/Cancelled Cheque)
Character Certificate From Last Educational Institution
Parents Consent Letter (For Residential Training)
Medical Insurance Details (If Any)

scheme_name: PMMSY: Refrigerated vehicles - Gujarat
details:
The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)” with the component of "Refrigerated vehicles", introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to modernise cold chain infrastructure to preserve fish health, uplift fishermen, and enhance value chain efficiency. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to fishermen and related beneficiaries for constructing ice plants or storage units with a minimum capacity of 30 tonnes, ensuring hygienic fish storage and better returns. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from the State Government.
﻿

Objective of the scheme
Marine production in the Gujarat state is one of the leading in the country. The state lags behind in exports due to non-norms. So cold storage/ice plant modernization is necessary to keep the fish in healthy condition.
Insulated vehicle, three wheeler with ice box including e-rickshaw, and live fish vending center to deliver the fish in hygienic condition to the consumer, so that the consumer (customer) can get fresh and clean fish from the local area and get good returns to the sellers.
Around 27,000 small and large boats are operating in the state. To strengthen the safety and security of the fishermen and increase the income of the boat owner, replacement of the existing boats is necessary to increase the sea growth of the existing boats.
benefits:
The unit cost of a refrigerated vehicle is ₹25,00,000/-.
40% assistance (up to ₹10,00,000/-) is provided for general category beneficiaries.
60% assistance (up to ₹15,00,000/-) is provided for women and SC/ST category beneficiaries.
﻿

Conditions:
The benefit will be available as per the target (category) allocated by the Government of India.
The benefit is available once in a lifetime.
﻿

Mode of Disbursement: Contribution is shared: 60% by the Government of India and 40% by State Governments.
Frequency of Disbursement: One-time assistance.
Validity: Once in a lifetime for eligible beneficiaries.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state.
The applicant should have a fishing boat registered under Real Craft software.
The applicant should hold a fishing license.
The applicant should complete the online registration on the iKhedut Portal.
The applicant should obtain in-principle approval before purchase of the floor plan.
The applicant should have his/her own bank account.
﻿

Preference/Reservation/Relaxation:
Women, SC, and ST beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost.
General category beneficiaries are eligible for 40% assistance of the unit cost.
application_process:
Online
Registration Process:
Step 1: The applicant may visit and apply online at the I-Khedut Portal.
Step 2: Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.
Step 3: Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Step 4: Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
﻿

Login Process: 
Step 1: The applicant may visit and apply online at the I-Khedut Portal.
Step 2: Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Step 3: Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Step 4: Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Step 5: Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Step 6: Once the application is done, confirm it.
Step 7: Take a printout of the confirmed application.
﻿

Contact Us: Click here.
