scheme_name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for the Students of Economically Backward Class
details: The "Pre-Matric Scholarship for the Students of Economically Backward Class" was launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance to school-going students from economically backward sections to reduce the dropout rate and promote education from class 1st to 10th. Under this scheme, monetary scholarships are provided to boys and girls studying in government and aided primary schools from Standard 1st to 10th, based on class and gender.
benefits:
Scholarship Amounts:
Boys (Std. 1st to 8th): ₹750/- per annum.
Boys (Std. 9th to 10th): ₹1,000/- per annum.
Girls (Std. 1st to 5th): ₹500/- per annum.
Girls (Std. 6th to 10th): ₹1,000/- per annum.
eligibility:
The applicant must be studying in a government or aided primary/secondary school in Gujarat.
The applicant must belong to an Economically Backward Class (EBC).
The applicant must be studying in classes 1st to 10th.
There is no income limit for students of classes 1st to 10th.
The applicant’s annual family income must not exceed ₹6,00,000/- for classes 9th to 10th.
application_process:
Online
Application Process:
Step 01: The applicant may visit the Digital Gujarat Portal: https://www.digitalgujarat.gov.in/﻿
Step 02: On the home page, click on ‘Register’ and the applicant will be redirected to the registration form page.
Step 03: Enter your Mobile Number, Email, Set Password, and then click on ‘Save’.
Step 04: After successful registration, the applicant can login through his/her User Name (Mobile No./Email ID) and Password.
Step 05: Now, click on ‘My Profile’ to update your profile and fill in all the mandatory information.
Step 06: After filling in profile details, click on ‘Update Profile’. The profile will be updated after entering the verification Code (OTP).
Step 07: Now under services, select ‘Scholarship Services’ and then click on ‘SC Scheme’.
Step 08: Select the Financial Year for which you want to apply for and then click on ‘Change Financial Year’.
Step 09: Select the scheme, read the interaction carefully, and then click on ‘Continue to Service’.
Step 10: Fill in the application form completely and upload all the relevant documents.
Step 11: Now, click on ‘Save as Draft’ and agree with the terms & conditions, and submit the application form by clicking on ‘Confirm & Final Submit’. 
﻿

Instructions: 
Note 01: Photo uploading for all applicants is mandatory.
Note 02: Application will be considered complete only when all sections are completed.
Note 03: Only one application per applicant will be acceptable for a scholarship. If any student submits more than one application either from a single institute or from multiple institutions his/her all applications will be rejected by the competent authority and no request from any side will be entertained.
Note 04: In case of any wrong/misleading information provided in the application shall lead to rejection of the application by Department Authorities.
Note 05: All required documents must be carefully scanned as per the instructions given.
Note 06: The student has to fill his/her application carefully as there is no provision for institution change once the application is submitted successfully.
Note 07: The unreadable uploaded documents will allow the competent authority to reject the student application.
Note 08: Direct Individual submission of this application form will not be accepted in case of students studying inside the state. Only outside students have to fill out their form online and submit his/her application form to their native district officer.

scheme_name: Assistance for Exhibitions Abroad and Out of State
details: The scheme "Assistance for Exhibitions Abroad and Out of State" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat aims to encourage and promote artists originally from Gujarat in the field of fine arts—such as painting, sculpture, graphic arts, and photography—by offering financial assistance to organize exhibitions abroad and outside the state. The scheme ensures support for artists with a record of conducting prior solo or group exhibitions, helping them to gain national and international exposure through structured financial aid. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat State Fine Arts Academy.
benefits:
Financial Assistance: Up to ₹1,00,000/- for organizing exhibitions abroad. 
Particulars	Maximum Allowable Amount
Gallery rent and decoration	15,000/-
Transportation of artworks	10,000/-
Publication of catalogues or printed materials related to the exhibition	₹25,000/-
Framing, mounting, and display preparation of artworks	₹20,000/-
Inaugural ceremony, publicity, and advertisement costs	₹15,000/-
Artist’s accommodation, travel, food, local transport, and miscellaneous	₹15,000/-
eligibility:
The applicant should be an artist originally from Gujarat.
The applicant must have organized either:
Individual Artists: Artists practicing in fields of visual arts (painting, sculpture, printmaking, etc.) who wish to participate in or organize exhibitions outside Gujarat or internationally.
Registered Art Organizations or Groups: Art societies, academies, educational institutions, or cultural organizations that promote visual arts and are organizing such exhibitions.
Applicants must exhibit a minimum of 30 original artworks at the exhibition for which financial assistance is being sought
The exhibition must be:
Outside the State (interstate exhibitions), or
Outside the Country (international exhibitions abroad), and
Must be art-related, such as showcasing paintings, sculptures, mixed media, or visual art forms.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Establishment of Large RAS Culture System Gujarat
details:
The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)" with the component of the Establishment of Large RAS (with 8 tanks of minimum 90 m3/ tank capacity 40 tonnes/ crop) Culture System, introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided for various inland fisheries activities, including the establishment of large Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS). The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
﻿

Objective: 
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries. 
Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits:
Assistance is provided for the establishment of a Large Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) with 8 tanks of a minimum 90 m3/tank capacity, targeting 40 tonnes/crop production.
The unit cost for this component is ₹50,00,000/- per unit.
For the General Category, the scale of assistance is 40% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
For Women, Scheduled Castes (SC), and Scheduled Tribes (ST) categories, the scale of assistance is 60% of the unit cost or 60% of the actual cost, whichever is less. 
﻿

Mode of Disbursement: 60% of the government contribution is from the Government of India, and 40% is from the State Government.
Frequency of Disbursement: The minimum time limit for reclaiming benefits is specified as "Once in a lifetime".
eligibility:
The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.
The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
﻿

Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

Step 3: Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Step 4: Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Establishment of Medium RAS Culture System Gujarat
details:
The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)" with a component Establishment of Medium RAS (with 6 tanks of minimum 30m3/tank capacity 10t/crop) Culture System, introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities. Through this scheme, financial assistance is provided to individuals and entities for the establishment of Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) based medium-scale fish tanks using limited land and water resources. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
﻿

Objective: 
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries. 
Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits:
The scheme provides assistance for the establishment of a Medium Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS) with 6 tanks of a minimum 30m3/tank capacity (10t/crop). 
Financial Assistance:
The unit cost for this component is ₹25,00,000/- per unit.
General Category: 40% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less, is paid as subsidy.
Female/Scheduled Caste (SC)/Scheduled Tribe (ST) Category: 60% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less, is paid as subsidy.
﻿

Frequency of Disbursement: The benefit for the establishment of a Medium RAS (with 6 tanks of minimum 30m3/tank capacity 10t/crop) culture system (PMMSY) can be reclaimed once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.
The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
﻿

Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

scheme_name: Sub mission on Seeds and Planting Material
details:
The scheme "Sub-mission on Seeds and Planting Material" by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to achieve several key objectives. Through this scheme, assistance for seed distribution, strengthening of seed quality control laboratories, and creation of seed infrastructure facilities are provided to all categories of farmers, State Agricultural Universities (SAUs), Farmers' Producer Organisations (FPOs), Co-operative enterprises, and Government enterprises. 
﻿

Objective of Scheme:
Increasing production of certified/quality seed
Strengthening Seed Testing Laboratories
Assisting Seed Producing Institutes for the adoption of new technologies, ensuring farmers get quality seeds at the right time and at reasonable prices
Development of infrastructure for seed industries
Improving seed quality
Enhancing capacity building for seed production
Creation of a Seed Bank for adverse natural calamities
benefits:
Seed Village Programme:
Assistance will be provided for the distribution of foundation and certified seed required for a 1-acre area for cereal crops; the assistance covers 50% of the cost, and for pulse, oilseeds, green manure & fodder crops, the assistance covers 60% of the cost.
Validity: The maximum time limit for reclaiming benefits is 2 years.
Strengthening of Seed Quality Control Laboratories:
Assistance is provided on a project basis.
Intended Use: For State Agricultural Universities (SAUs), Farmers' Producer Organisations (FPOs), Co-operative enterprises, and Government enterprises.
Frequency of Disbursement: One time.
Creation of Seed Infrastructure Facility (SPP):
Assistance is provided on a project basis.
eligibility:

scheme_name: Mission for Integrated Horticulture Development (MIDH)
details: The scheme "Mission for Integrated Horticulture Development (MIDH)" is a centrally sponsored scheme that aims to ensure the holistic development of horticulture by providing financial assistance and technical support to farmers for the cultivation of fruits, vegetables, spices, flowers, and other horticultural crops, along with promoting modern technologies, post-harvest management, and value addition. The scheme promotes cluster planting, renovation of old gardens, use of advanced technologies such as protected cultivation, integrated pest and nutrient management, and horticultural mechanization to enhance production and sustainability in alignment with regional agro-climatic conditions.
benefits:
Crop Development and Production: Support programs are provided for planting and increasing the production of diverse horticultural crops, including fruits, vegetables, pepper, spices, and flower crops, tailored to different regions and agro-climates within the state.
Technological Promotion: The scheme promotes and extends the use of modern technology in horticulture.
Propagation Support: Support programs are available for grafting and stem raising, contributing to better crop propagation.
Post-Harvest Management and Value Addition: Assistance is provided for post-harvest management processes and value addition initiatives, ensuring better returns for produce.
Market System Development: Financial assistance is extended to individuals and the private sector for the development of collection and market systems, aiming for comprehensive growth in horticulture.
Specific Component Inclusion: The scheme primarily includes components such as- Cluster planting of new fruit, flower, vegetable, aromatic, and spice crops; Renovation of old gardens; Horticultural mechanization; Integrated fertilizer pest control management; Protected cultivation for horticultural crops; Mulching and the use of bird nets, etc.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a farmer
 The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should intend to cultivate horticultural crops or undertake related activities.

scheme_name: Parivahan Scheme (GBCDC)
details:
The scheme "Parivahan Scheme (GBCDC)" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance for the purchase of vehicles in the transport sector to promote self-employment among the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC).
Through this scheme, a loan is provided to unemployed individuals for purchasing vehicles like auto-rickshaws, loading vehicles, etc. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat Backward Classes Development Corporation (GBCDC).
benefits: The applicant can avail a loan of up to ₹2,00,000/- for purchasing vehicles such as auto-rickshaws, loading vehicles, and other transport vehicles.
eligibility:
The applicant should belong to the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) category.
The applicant can be of any gender.
The applicant should be unemployed.
The applicant should have any level of education.
The applicant should have an individual and family annual income up to ₹3,00,000/-.
The applicant should hold a valid Driving Licence for the type of vehicle intended for purchase (specifically for passenger vehicles).
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Visit the official website of the Gujarat Backwards Caste Development Corporation.
Step 2: On the top menu bar, click on "Apply Online".
Step 3: If you’re a new user, click on "New Registration" Enter personal details (name, mobile number, email, aadhaar number, etc.), create a password, and complete OTP verification.
Step 4: Use your registered credentials (User ID and password) to log in.
Step 5: Click on “New Application”. Select “Parivahan Scheme” from the list of available schemes.
Step 6: Fill out the Application Form, enter the following details:
Personal Information: Name, gender, date of birth, caste, etc.
Address Information: Permanent and correspondence address
Income Details: Monthly family income and source
Loan Requirement: Amount needed, proposed use (e.g., business, self-employment)
Bank Account Details: For loan disbursement
Step 7: Upload scanned copies of the Caste certificate, Income certificate, Aadhaar card, Passport-size photo, Bank passbook (first page), and Residence proof.
Step 8: Double-check all details. Click on “Preview” to verify. If all details are correct, click “Submit”.
Step 9: You will receive an Application ID/Reference Number. Save or print the acknowledgement for future tracking.

scheme_name: Advocate Graduate Stipend Gujarat
details: The “Advocate Graduate Stipend” scheme was launched by the Tribal Development Department, Government of Gujarat. It aims to provide financial assistance and practical training support to tribal law graduates who begin their legal practice. Under this scheme, tribal youth who have obtained their charter within two years of completing their LLB are given a monthly stipend.
benefits:
Stipend to junior tribal advocates:
₹10,000 per month for the first year.
₹8,000 per month for the second year.
₹6,000 per month for the third year.
Allowance to senior advocate (mentor): 
₹5,000 per month for three years.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a Scheduled Tribe (ST) youth.
The applicant should have completed LLB degree.
The applicant should have obtained a charter within two years of graduation.
The applicant should begin legal practice as a junior lawyer under a senior advocate.
The applicant should be practicing in tribal areas of Gujarat.

scheme_name: Project Sankalp
details: The scheme "Project Sankalp" was launched on 15th July 2020 by the Gujarat Skill Development Mission (GSDM), Labour and Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to revolutionize vocational skilling, making India the global skill capital with globally competitive skills and competencies. The scheme provides an industry-led vocational skilling program, enabling the industry to fulfill its demand for skilled manpower and providing youth with sustainable employment opportunities. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat Skill Development Mission. The applications for this scheme are accepted online through the GSDM portal.
benefits:
Training Cost Reimbursement 
The Industry Partner will receive reimbursement for training costs per candidate per hour:
NSQF Category 1 Job Roles: ₹49.
NSQF Category 2 Job Roles: ₹42.
NSQF Category 3 Job Roles: ₹35.10.
GCVT Job Roles and Company-Specific Job Roles: ₹36.75 (which is 75% of the Category 1 NSQF job role training cost).
*The training cost for NSQF Job Roles is subject to amendments as per MSDE notification, and the cost for GCVT and Company-specific job roles will also change accordingly.
Assessment Cost Reimbursement
GSDM will reimburse the assessment cost directly to the respective Sector Skill Council (SSC), Gujarat Council of Vocational Training (GCVT), or Kaushalya Skill University (KSU) for both fresher trainees and existing employees.
*Any re-assessment cost for candidates who were absent or failed the first assessment, or for existing workers/employees, must be borne by the Industry Partner.
No Training Fee for Youth/Beneficiaries
No fee of any kind will be charged from the youth or beneficiaries for the training.
eligibility:
The Fresher Trainees must have a domicile of Gujarat.
The age of Fresher Trainees must be between 18 and 35 years.
The Existing Employees could be from Pan-India.
The age of Existing Employees must be between 18 and 55 years.
Relaxations & Preferences
The scheme focuses on the inclusion of Special Groups such as Women, Persons with Disability (PwD), Below Poverty Line (BPL), Scheduled Caste (SC), and Scheduled Tribe (ST).
A relaxation of ten years may be given to Women, PwD, BPL, SC, and ST candidates for fresher trainees.
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Access the GSDM Portal & Initiate Registration
To begin, the applicant should visit the Skills Gujarat portal. On the displayed login screen, the applicant must locate and click on the "Register". This action will prompt the system to initiate the candidate registration process, allowing new users to create an account.
Step 2: Complete Basic Registration Details
The applicant will be required to provide all the mandatory details in the registration form. 
The system will generate One Time Passwords (OTPs), which will be sent to both the registered email ID and the mobile number provided by the applicant. 
A verification screen will appear, where the applicant must accurately enter the "Email OTP" and "Mobile OTP" received.
Step 3: Confirm Verification and Receive Login Credentials
After entering both OTPs, the applicant should click the "Verify & Save" button. If the OTPs are successfully verified, a confirmation message will appear, indicating successful basic registration. 
Subsequently, the "User Name" and "Password" for the account will be generated and shared with the applicant via their registered email ID and mobile number.
Step 4: Initial Login and Password Creation
Using the newly received "Username" and "Password," along with the Captcha displayed on the login screen, the applicant should log in to their account. 
For security purposes, the system will then prompt the applicant to create a new password by entering a "New Password" and re-entering it in the "Enter Confirm Password" field, followed by another Captcha. 
The applicant must click "Submit" to finalize the new password.
Step 5: Complete Full Registration
Once logged in with the new password, the applicant will proceed to the Full Registration section. 
Here, they must provide all the mandatory details and upload the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. 
The applicant must carefully read and then click "I Agree to the Terms and Conditions" to confirm the correctness of all the information updated during the registration process. 
Finally, they should click "Save and Continue" to submit their complete registration details.
Step 6: Access Candidate Dashboard and Utilize Resources
Upon successful full registration, the applicant will be directed to their Candidate Dashboard. 
From this dashboard, they can access various features, including the "TC Locator / KVK Locator" to search for the Nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) or Training Center. 
Additionally, they can click on "Interest Assessment Test," which redirects them to http://gujarati.careerguide.com/my-profile, to start and view their psychometric test report.
*No fee of any kind should be charged from the youth or beneficiary for the Project Sankalp training.
documents_required:
Aadhaar ID
Identity Proof (for verification; original documents will not be retained by Training Providers/Training Centres)
Address Proof (for verification; original documents will not be retained by Training Providers/Training Centres)

scheme_name: Gujarat State Electric Vehicle Policy
details: The "Gujarat State Electric Vehicle Policy 2021" by the Ports and Transport Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to transition the state's transportation sector towards electric mobility, make Gujarat a manufacturing hub for electric vehicles and ancillary equipment, encourage start-ups and investment in the field of electric mobility, and improve environmental quality by reducing air pollution. The policy provides capital subsidies up to ₹10,000/- per kilowatt hour for electric vehicle purchases and 25% capital subsidy on equipment for charging infrastructure limited up to ₹10,00,000/- per station for the first 250 commercial public electric vehicle charging stations. The policy is implemented by the Ports and Transport Department as the nodal department with Energy and Petrochemicals Department handling charging station subsidies.
benefits:
Electric Vehicle Purchase Incentives
2-Wheeler: ₹10,000/- per kilowatt hour (maximum ex-factory price ₹1,50,000/-)
3-Wheeler: ₹10,000/- per kilowatt hour (maximum ex-factory price ₹5,00,000/-)
4-Wheeler: ₹10,000/- per kilowatt hour (maximum ex-factory price ₹15,00,000/-)
Charging Infrastructure Incentives
Capital Subsidy: 25% on equipment/machinery limited up to ₹10,00,000/- per station
Electricity Duty Exemption: 100% exemption for electric vehicle charging stations during policy period
Eligibility: First 250 commercial public electric vehicle charging stations
Manufacturing Benefits
All provisions of Gujarat Industrial Policy-2020 applicable to electric vehicle manufacturing sector
Support for setting up or upgrading facilities for electric vehicle manufacturing
Conditions
*The beneficiary can avail subsidy under only one state government scheme and cannot claim subsidy under multiple schemes.
*Maximum subsidy amount is limited to maximum battery capacity of the vehicle.
*Maximum subsidy should not exceed 40% of ex-factory price of the vehicle.
*The amount whichever is less will be given as subsidy to the beneficiary.
*Charging stations must adhere to Ministry of Power guidelines and standards dated 01.10.2019.
*Charging station subsidy available only to those who have not availed subsidies under any other Government of India schemes.
eligibility:
Electric Vehicle Purchase Subsidy
The applicant must purchase electric vehicles that have taken subsidy under Government of India's Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Hybrid and Electric Vehicles in India Scheme dated 8th March 2019 and any amendments thereafter.
The applicant must ensure the vehicle's ex-factory price falls within the prescribed threshold limits for each segment.
The applicant must not have claimed subsidy under any other state government scheme run by different departments.
The applicant must authenticate documents for vehicle purchase to receive Direct Benefit Transfer.
Charging Infrastructure Subsidy
The applicant must set up commercial public electric vehicle charging stations for 2-wheelers, 3-wheelers, and 4-wheelers.
The applicant must not have availed subsidies under any other promotional schemes under Government of India policies or schemes.
The applicant must ensure charging stations meet guidelines and standards of Ministry of Power Circular dated 1st October 2019 and any amendments thereafter.
The applicant must be among the first 250 commercial public electric vehicle charging station developers.
Manufacturing Incentives
The applicant must be involved in manufacturing electric vehicles and their components.
The applicant must comply with provisions of Gujarat Industrial Policy-2020 and subsequent applicable policies.
application_process:
Offline
Upon authenticating the document for the purchase of the vehicle, the subsidy shall be disbursed directly to the customer via DBT mode from the State Transport Department.
The subsidy amount will be calculated based on kilowatt-hour capacity for vehicles or a 25% capital subsidy for charging infrastructure, subject to maximum limits and eligibility conditions.
documents_required:

Commercial Public Charging Station Registration Documents
For Manufacturing Incentives
Gujarat Industrial Policy-2020 Compliance Documents
Manufacturing License and Registrations

Investment and Setup Documentation

scheme_name: Interest Subsidy Scheme on Education Loan
details: The scheme "Interest Subsidy Scheme on Education Loan" by the Education Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide complete financial relief to students through 100% interest subsidy on education loans for the entire moratorium period. This includes the study period and 1 additional year after course completion.
benefits: Interest Subsidy Assistance: 100% interest subsidy on education loans up to ₹10,00,000/- for the moratorium period (course duration + 1 year).
eligibility:

The applicant must be a student.
The applicant must have passed HSC (Higher Secondary Certificate) with at least 60 percentile.
The applicant must have taken an education loan from a recognized bank for pursuing higher education in India or abroad.
The applicant must have a family income not exceeding ₹6,00,000/- per year.

scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Gyansetu Merit Scholarship Yojana
details: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Gyansetu Merit Scholarship Yojana" by the Education Department, Government of Gujarat aims to select 30,000 brilliant students who have completed their Class 5 education consecutively in government, aided, or RTE-approved schools and provide them with scholarships to study in selected government, aided, or private schools from Class 6th to Class 12th. The selection is done through a Common Entrance Test conducted by the State Examination Board, Gandhinagar.
benefits:
Government Schools and Government -aided Schools (Approved by Education Department):
For Class 6th to 8th: ₹5,000/- per year.
For Class 9th to 10th: ₹6,000/- per year.
For Class 11th to 12th: ₹7,000/- per year.
Private (Selected) Schools:
For Class 6th to 8th: ₹20,000/- per year.
For Class 9th to 10th: ₹22,000/- per year.
For Class 11th to 12th: ₹25,000/- per year.
Frequency of Disbursement: 
Disbursed annually in two installments; the first 50% is given after confirmation of at least 80% attendance in the first semester.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a student.
The applicant must have studied Classes 1st to 5th in a Government/ Government-aided/ RTE-approved self-financed primary school in Gujarat.
The applicant must have passed the Common Entrance Test (CET) conducted by the State Examination Board and be listed in the merit list.
The applicant must have taken admission in Class 6th in a Government/ Government-aided/ RTE-approved self-financed primary school in Gujarat.
application_process:
Online

Step 1: Visit the official website: http://gssyguj.in.
Step 2: Register online using the 18-digit CTS Aadhaar UDI number.

Application Process:

scheme_name: NAMO Shree Scheme
details: Launched in 2024, the scheme "NAMO Shree Scheme" by the Women and Child Development Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to ensure that every pregnant woman and lactating mother of the state gets proper nutritional support and also encourages them to have institutional deliveries for women's empowerment and improving maternal health. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹12,000/- to eligible women beneficiaries and covers expenses from conception to delivery of the child. The applicant must be Gujarat's pregnant woman or a lactating mother, whose family income is not more than ₹8,00,000/- per year, and must deliver a child in any government or private hospital. The scheme is implemented by the Women and Child Development Department of the Gujarat Government, and registration under this scheme is done at the time of the first antenatal check-up through an Accredited Social Health Activist or Auxiliary Nurse Midwife Worker at the nearest Anganwadi Centre.
benefits:
Financial Assistance Amount
Eligible women beneficiaries receive ₹12,000/- from conception to delivery of the child for their first two live children, distributed in phases.
﻿

Disbursal for First Pregnancy
₹5,000/- at the time of registration (₹2,000/- from State Government and ₹3,000/- from Central Government)
₹2,000/- after six months of pregnancy (from the State Government)
₹3,000/- immediately after institutional delivery (from the State Government)
₹2,000/- after the 14-week vaccination (from the Central Government)
﻿

Disbursal for Second Pregnancy
₹2,000/- at the time of registration
₹3,000/- after six months of pregnancy
₹6,000/- after institutional delivery (amount given by the Central Government if the newborn is a girl and by the State Government if the newborn is a boy)
₹1,000/- from the State Government after the 14-week vaccination
﻿

Mode of Disbursement: The Amount is transferred directly into the beneficiary woman's bank account through installments.
Additional Benefits: Beneficiary women are also eligible to avail the benefits of other Central Government schemes like Pradhan Mantri Matritva Vandana Yojana and Janani Suraksha Yojana.
﻿

Conditions for Availment
A certificate from the Medical Institution of Delivery is required for the last installment.
eligibility:
1. The applicant must be from Gujarat.
2. The applicant must be a pregnant woman or a lactating mother.
3. The applicant must belong to one of the following categories:
Scheduled Tribe
Scheduled Caste
Beneficiary of the National Food Security Act
Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana Beneficiary
Below Poverty Line families
E Shram Card holder women beneficiary
Women who are farmers
Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act beneficiary
4. The applicant's family income must not be more than ₹8,00,000/- per year.
5. The applicant must deliver the child in any government or private hospital.
6. The applicant must register at the time of the first antenatal check-up.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: Visit the Nearest Anganwadi Centre
The applicant must visit their nearest Anganwadi Centre to initiate the registration process for the NAMO Shree Scheme.
Step 2: Registration by Accredited Social Health Activist or Auxiliary Nurse Midwife Worker
The Accredited Social Health Activist or Auxiliary Nurse Midwife Worker at the Anganwadi Centre will register the pregnant woman in the NAMO Shree Scheme. The registration must be completed at the time of the first antenatal check-up.
Step 3: Final Approval Process
The final approval for the scheme benefits will be provided by the Women and Child Development Department of the Government of Gujarat after processing the registration.
Step 4: Benefit Disbursement
Once approved, the financial assistance amount will be disbursed in installments directly into the beneficiary's bank account as per the scheme guidelines.
*For receiving the final installment, the beneficiary must submit a Certificate from the Medical Institution of Delivery to complete the benefit disbursement process.
*Applicants are advised to save copies of all submitted documents and acknowledgements for future reference.
documents_required:
At the Time of Application
Aadhaar Card
Details of Bank Account
Mobile Number
MAMTA Card
For the Last Installment
Certificate from the Medical Institution of Delivery

scheme_name: Silicosis Financial Assistance Scheme
details: Launched in 2006, the scheme "Silicosis Financial Assistance Scheme" by the Gujarat Rural Workers Welfare Board, Labour and Employment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to provide financial assistance to the heirs of self-reliant persons carrying on occupation of Akik who suffer from the serious disease of silicosis in the state, addressing the occupational health hazards faced by workers in the Akik industry. The scheme provides financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- to the heirs of affected workers as a security measure and compensation for the occupational disease. The applicant must be the heir of a self-reliant person who was engaged in Akik occupation and died due to silicosis disease in Gujarat state. The scheme is implemented by Gujarat Rural Workers Welfare Board under Labour and Employment Department. The applications for this scheme are accepted through the prescribed process.
benefits:
Financial Assistance: ₹1,00,000/- (one lakh rupees) per case.
Purpose: Financial assistance to heirs of deceased workers who died due to silicosis.
Nature: One-time payment as compensation.
eligibility:
The applicant must be the legal heir of a deceased person who was engaged in Akik occupation.
The applicant must be the heir of a self-reliant person who carried on occupation of Akik in Gujarat state.
The applicant must be the heir of a person who died due to serious disease of silicosis.
The applicant must be the heir of a worker whose death occurred due to occupational silicosis while engaged in Akik work.
The applicant must establish the occupational nature of the silicosis disease in relation to Akik work.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The interested applicant should visit the Office of the Government Labour Officer, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the authority given below - 
Office of the Government Labour Officer (Rural) in the case of worker living in rural area.
Office of the Government Labour Officer (Industry) in the case of worker living in urban area.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
Step 5: The concerned authorities will verify the submitted documents and establish the eligibility of the applicant as legal heir and confirm the occupational nature of silicosis disease.
Step 6: The board will assess the case to confirm that the deceased was engaged in Akik occupation and that the death was due to silicosis disease as per medical evidence.
Step 7: Upon successful verification and assessment, the case will be processed for approval and financial sanction by the competent authority.
documents_required:
Death Certificate of The Worker
Medical Certificate Confirming Silicosis as Cause of Death
Legal Heir Certificate
Proof of Employment in Akik Occupation
Identity Proof of The Applicant (Heir)
Address Proof of The Applicant
Medical Records Establishing Silicosis Disease
Employment Records or Proof of Akik Occupation of The Deceased

scheme_name: Namo Laxmi Yojana
details: The scheme "Namo Laxmi Yojana" by the Education Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to encourage girls studying in government, aided, and private schools in Gujarat to complete their education from Class 9th to 12th by providing direct financial assistance. Each eligible girl will receive a total financial support of ₹50,000/- on completion of classes 9th to 12th under this scheme.
benefits:
Total Financial Assistance:
Each eligible girl will receive a total financial support of ₹50,000/- on completion of classes 9th to 12th in government, aided, and private schools.
﻿

Class 9th-10th Benefit:
₹10,000/- per annum (₹500/- per month based on attendance × 10) and the remaining 50% will be credited via DBT (Direct Benefit Transfer) after passing the Class 10th board examination.
﻿

Class 11th-12th Benefit:
₹15,000/- per annum (₹750/- per month based on attendance × 10) and the remaining 50% will be credited via DBT after passing the Class 12th board examination.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a girl.
The applicant must be a student.
The applicant must be studying in Class 9th to 12th in any Government, Government-aided, or private secondary/higher secondary school in Gujarat.
The applicant must have an annual family income of less than or equal to ₹6,00,000/-.
The applicant must maintain regular attendance to be eligible for monthly assistance.
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Applicants should request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the School authority.
Step 2: In the application form, provide all the mandatory details, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the supporting documents to the District Education Officer.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted.
documents_required: Income Certificate.

scheme_name: Scheduled Caste Sub Plan (SCSP): Electrification of Wells of Scheduled Caste Farmers
details: The scheme "Scheduled Caste Sub Plan (SCSP): Electrification of Wells of Scheduled Caste Farmers" was launched by the Energy and Petrochemicals Department, Government of Gujarat, with financial allocation starting from the year 2016-17, to provide agricultural electricity connection to Scheduled Caste farmers on a priority basis. The scheme provides electrification of agriculture wells of Scheduled Caste Farmers. Free domestic electricity connections are also provided to Scheduled Caste beneficiaries. The applicant must be a Scheduled Caste farmer. The applications for this scheme are accepted at the Subdivisional Office or via the All Discom Website.
benefits: Electrification of agriculture wells of the farmer.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a farmer.
The applicant must be from a Scheduled Caste.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The application form required for this scheme can be obtained free of cost from the Discom Website. Alternatively, applicants may collect physical forms from their nearest Subdivisional Office.
Step 2: Once the form is fully filled and all required documents are attached, the complete application must be submitted to the Subdivisional Office. 
Step 3: The applicants are mandated to pay the Registration Charge, Security Deposit, Agreement Fee, and Test report fee for the agricultural connection application.
*The capital costs associated with High Tension (HT) lines, Low Tension (LT) lines, and Transformer centers are waived for applicants under this scheme.
documents_required:
Copy of Charter of Record 7/12, 8-A and 6-A.
Proof of Identity.
Gender Certificate.
In case of co-owners/partners, a consent letter on notarized stamp paper as per charter of 7/12 and 6-A.
Caste Certificate (obtained from the Social Welfare Department).

scheme_name: Manav Garima Yojna SCW (EBC)
details: The “Manav Garima Yojna SCW (EBC)" scheme is launched by the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat. It aims to help economically backward class (EBC) people live with dignity, become self-employed in small businesses, and achieve financial self-sufficiency. Through this scheme, various business tool-kits are provided to members of the economically backward class so they can start income-generating activities and reduce dependence on wage labour.
benefits:
The primary benefit is the provision of various business tool-kits to eligible beneficiaries so that they can engage in small business activities and become self-employed and financially self-sufficient.
The tool kits are given under the scheme targeted at the Economically Backward Class (EBC).
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must belong to the Economically Backward Class (EBC).
The applicant’s annual individual and family income should not exceed ₹6,00,000/-.
application_process:
Online
Step 01: The eligible applicant may visit the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal: https://esamajkalyan.gujarat.gov.in/﻿
Step 02: On the home page, under the tab ‘Citizen Login’, click on New User ‘Please Register Here’.
Step 03: Enter your Full Name as per Aadhaar Card, Gender, Date of Birth, Aadhaar Card Number, Email ID, Caste, etc. and then click on ‘Register’.
Step 04: After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
Step 05: Now, click on ‘User Profile’ to update your profile.
Step 06: Fill in all the mandatory information and then click on ‘Update’. 
Step 07: After updating the profile successfully, select the scheme appearing on the home page; an application form will open.
Step 08: Fill in all the mandatory details in the application form and upload all the relevant documents.
Step 09: Now agree with the terms & conditions and click on ‘Save Application’.
Step 10: Submit the application form and take out the print of the application for future reference. The applicant can note down the application number to track the application status by logging into the portal using their User ID and Password.
﻿User Manual

scheme_name: 100% Penalty Mafi Yojana
details: The scheme "100% Penalty Mafi Yojana" was launched by the Urban Development and Urban Housing Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide financial relief to beneficiaries of the Gujarat Housing Board and Slum Clearance Cell who have pending installment dues by offering a complete waiver of penalty amounts, thereby reducing the financial burden on property allottees and encouraging them to clear their outstanding dues. The scheme provides 100% waiver on penalty amounts if all arrears of installments are paid in one go by 31st March and facilitates the transfer of property ownership to eligible applicants. The applications for this scheme are accepted Online.
benefits:
The applicant receives 100% waiver on the penalty amount accumulated on pending installments.
The waiver is applicable on all arrears of installments pertaining to old schemes of the Gujarat Housing Board and Slum Clearance Cell.
The benefit is a one-time waiver granted upon fulfillment of payment conditions.
The waiver is applied at the time of final settlement of dues.
Conditions
*All arrears of installments must be paid in one go (lump sum payment).
*The payment must be completed by 31st March.
*The waiver applies only to the penalty component; the principal installment amount must be paid in full.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a beneficiary of the Gujarat Housing Board or Slum Clearance Cell.
The applicant must have pending installment arrears under the old schemes of the Gujarat Housing Board and Slum Clearance Cell.
The applicant must clear all arrears of installments in one lump sum payment.
The applicant must complete the payment by 31st March.
The applicant must be either the original tenant, a successive power of attorney holder, or the existing occupant of the property.
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Access the online application form. Enter all mandatory personal information. Verify that all mandatory fields marked with asterisk (*) have been filled. 
Step 2: For document upload, click "Choose File" or drag and drop documents into appropriate upload fields. Ensure all documents are clear, legible, and within the specified file size limits.
Step 3: Carefully review all entered information and uploaded documents for accuracy and completeness. Click "Submit". Note down any application reference number or acknowledgment number provided for future tracking and correspondence.
Step 4: After successful application submission, proceed to pay all pending arrears of installments in one lump sum payment through designated Gujarat Housing Board payment channels by 31st March. Retain payment receipts for record and verification purposes.
Application Deadline
The critical deadline for completing the lump sum payment of all arrears is 31st March of the respective year.
documents_required:

Copy of All Power of Attorney and Agreements Successive to the Original Tenant.
Copy of Rent Agreement, Delivery of Rent, or Letter from the Landlord.
Death Certificate (In case of death of the original tenant or power of attorney holder).
Latest Photograph of the House.
Copy of the Municipality or Municipal Corporation's Last Year's Tax Bill of the Building.
Copy of the Search Report of the House or Flat from the Registration Office of the Locality or Document (Latest).
Xerox Copies of the Receipt of the Amount Previously Paid to the Gujarat Housing Board.
Firm Name on a Stamp Paper of ₹300/- (In case of death of the original tenant or power of attorney holder).
Title Consent Form on a Stamp Paper of ₹300/-.
*All documents except the latest photograph of the house must be submitted in PDF Format with a maximum file size of 10 MB.
*The photograph must be submitted in .jpg or .jpeg format with with a maximum file size of 10 MB.

scheme_name: Assistance Program for Expansion of Fruit Crop Cultivation
details: The scheme "Assistance Program for Expansion of Fruit Crop Cultivation" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat,  aims to encourage and support farmers in the state to adopt intensive farming practices and increase the cultivation of fruits such as mango, guava, pomegranate, lemon, banana (tissue culture), and papaya.
benefits:
For Intensive and Ultra-Intensive Farming of Mango, Guava, Pomegranate, Lemon, etc.:
Assistance of ₹30,000/- to ₹40,000/- per hectare.
Maximum up to 4 hectares per beneficiary.
﻿

For New Plantation of Banana Tissue Culture Crop:
40% of the incurred cost or maximum ₹50,000/- per hectare.
Maximum up to 4 hectares per beneficiary.
﻿

For New Plantation of Papaya Crop:
50% of the incurred cost or maximum ₹30,000/- per hectare.
Paid in two installments.
Maximum up to 4 hectares per beneficiary.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a farmer.
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be engaged in horticultural or fruit crop cultivation.

scheme_name: PMMSY: Insulated vehicles - Gujarat
details:
The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)” with the component of "Insulated vehicles", introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to modernise cold chain infrastructure to preserve fish health, uplift fishermen, and enhance value chain efficiency. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to fishermen and related beneficiaries for constructing ice plants or storage units with a minimum capacity of 30 tonnes, ensuring hygienic fish storage and better returns. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from the State Government.
﻿

Objective of the scheme
Marine production in the Gujarat state is one of the leading in the country. The state lags behind in exports due to non-norms. So cold storage/ice plant modernization is necessary to keep the fish in healthy condition.
Insulated vehicle, three wheeler with ice box including e-rickshaw, and live fish vending center to deliver the fish in hygienic condition to the consumer, so that the consumer (customer) can get fresh and clean fish from the local area and get good returns to the sellers.
Around 27,000 small and large boats are operating in the state. To strengthen the safety and security of the fishermen and increase the income of the boat owner, replacement of the existing boats is necessary to increase the sea growth of the existing boats.
benefits:
The unit cost is ₹20,00,000/-. 40% assistance, i.e. ₹ 8,00,000/- for the general category and 60% i.e. assistance up to ₹12,00,000/- for women and ST/SC category beneficiaries.
The benefit will be available as per the target (category) allocated by the Government of India.
﻿

Remarks: 60% contribution from Government of India and 40% contribution from State Governments is paid.
Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits: Once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state.
The applicant should have a fishing boat registered under Real Craft software.
The applicant should hold a fishing license.
The applicant should complete the online registration on the iKhedut Portal.
The applicant should obtain in-principle approval before purchase of the floor plan.
The applicant should have his/her own bank account.
﻿

Preference/Reservation/Relaxation:
Women, SC, and ST beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost.
General category beneficiaries are eligible for 40% assistance of the unit cost.

scheme_name: Scheme to Provide Electric Scooter to Disabled Person
details: The "Scheme to Provide Electric Scooter to Disabled Person" is a welfare scheme by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department of Gujarat. The scheme aims to promote mobility and independence for orthopedically disabled individuals in Gujarat by offering a financial subsidy. The beneficiary can purchase an electric scooter through an agency approved by JEDA. After verification, the financial assistance amounting to ₹25,000 (or 50% of the scooter and kit cost, whichever is lower) will be directly deposited into the beneficiary's bank account.
benefits: Financial Assistance: Up to the limit of 50% of the basic cost of the electric scooter + 50% of the disability kit or ₹25,000/-.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be between 18 to 50 years of age.
The applicant must have an orthopedic disability of 40% or more, certified by a civil surgeon.
The applicant must possess a Disability Identity Card issued by the Social Security Department.
The applicant must be listed in the BPL (Below Poverty Line) category with a score of 0 to 20.
The applicant must not have already availed benefits under this scheme in the last five years, unless the previously allotted electric scooter is damaged and the applicant remains otherwise eligible.
The applicant must purchase the electric scooter from an agency approved by JEDA at the government-approved price.
Note: The applicant will be given priority if their name appears in the SECC-2011 (Socio-Economic Caste Census) data.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: Applications will be received under this scheme by publishing an advertisement.
Step 2: Obtain and fill out the application form (available free of cost).
Step 3: Submit the completed application form with required documents to the district-level office.
Step 4: Eligible applicants are selected via computerized draw.

Proof of Residence (Ration Card / Electricity Bill / Driving License / Aadhaar Card / Electoral Card / any one of)

scheme_name: Scholarship to Disabled Students
details: The scheme "Scholarship to Disabled Students" by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to support the educational needs of students with benchmark disabilities of more than 40%, as per the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. Through this scheme, annual scholarships ranging from ₹1,500/- to ₹4,500/- are provided to students with disabilities studying from Class 1 onwards, depending on the level of education. The scheme is implemented by the Social Defense Division of the Social Justice and Empowerment Department. The objective is to encourage continued education among students with disabilities by providing financial assistance, especially for those who have achieved at least 40% marks in their last annual examination and possess a valid disability identity card.

₹1,500/- per year for students with disabilities studying in Class 1st to 8th.
₹2,000/- to ₹4,500 per year for students studying in Class 9th and above.

The applicant should be a student.

The applicant should have passed the last annual examination with a minimum of 40% marks.
The applicant should possess a valid PH (Person with Disability) Identity Card.
The applicant should be currently studying in a recognized educational institution.

category: Education & Learning, Banking,Financial Services and Insurance
details: The "New Akanksha Scheme (Educational Loan)" is implemented by the Gujarat Thakor and Koli Vikas Nigam (GTKVN) under the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide financial support for higher technical and professional education to students belonging to Thakor and Koli communities. Books, writing materials, necessary equipment and examination fees required for the course
Accommodation & food expenses
Travel and insurance premiums
Contribution:
90% by Central Corporation
5% by State Government
5% by the Beneficiary

Start of Repayment: Within 6 months from course completion or employment, whichever is earlier.

MCA (Master of Computer Applications or equivalent)

category: Agriculture,Rural & Environment, Banking,Financial Services and Insurance, Business & Entrepreneurship
details: The "Pashu Palan Yojana" is implemented by the Gujarat Thakor and Koli Vikas Nigam (GTKVN) under the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to strengthen the livelihoods of the Thakor and Koli communities by providing financial support for animal husbandry activities, such as dairy farming with cows and buffaloes. Under this scheme, loans are offered to purchase milch animals like cows and buffaloes, thereby promoting sustainable self-employment in agriculture-related sectors.
benefits:
The maximum loan limit under this scheme is up to ₹1,00,000/-.

details: The “Food Bill Assistance for Medical and Engineering Students of Nomadic Tribes and Denotified Tribes” scheme was launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to reduce the burden of living expenses for students belonging to the Nomadic Tribes and Denotified Tribes (NTDNT) communities who are pursuing full-time Medical or Engineering courses within the state. Under this scheme, eligible students receive a monthly food allowance of ₹1,500/- for up to 10 months each academic year to help cover mess or canteen expenses.
benefits: Monthly assistance up to ₹1,500/- for 10 months per academic year.

scheme_name: Shree Nanaji Deshmukh Awas Yojana (GBOCWWB)
details: The “Shree Nanaji Deshmukh Awas Yojana” is implemented by the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board (GBOCWWB), Labour, Skill Development & Employment Department, Government of Gujarat. In urban areas, where EWS/LIG houses have been allotted by the Local Self-Government Organization/Urban Development Authority and Gujarat Housing Board under the Chief Minister/Prime Minister Awas Yojana, the benefit of this scheme is given once per family to the construction worker registered in the board.
benefits: Under the scheme, financial assistance of ₹1,60,000/- is provided to registered construction workers.
eligibility:
The construction workers who are registered with the Gujarat Building and Other Construction Worker’s Welfare Board will be eligible to get the benefit of this scheme.
A house is allotted jointly to a registered construction worker husband or wife and either of the husband or wife is a registered construction worker will be eligible for the benefit of this scheme.
No land/property or building should be in the name of the registered construction worker husband or wife or any of their family members.
The benefit of this scheme is given once per family to the registered construction worker.
Note 01: The construction worker should be registered in this scheme for at least 2 years.
Note 02: Application for this scheme has to be made after one year of house allotment.
application_process:
Online
Application Process for ‘E-Nirman Card’ under the GBOCWWB:
Step 01: The applicant may visit the e-Nirman Portal: https://enirmanbocw.gujarat.gov.in/﻿
Step 02: On the home page, under the tab ‘Login to Portal’, click on ‘Please Register Here’.
Step 03: Enter all the mandatory information and then click on ‘Register’.
Step 04: After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
Step 05: Now, update your profile by entering all the necessary details as required and click on ‘Update’.
Step 06: Select the ‘Construction Works Form’ and fill out all the mandatory information in the form.
Step 07: Upload all the relevant documents, agree with the terms & conditions, and submit the form.
Step 08: Once the application has been submitted, an application Number is generated which can be kept for future reference.
Process to track Application Status:
Step 01: The applicant may visit the e-Nirman Portal: https://enirmanbocw.gujarat.gov.in/﻿
Step 02: On the home page, click on ‘View Citizen Application Status’.
Step 03: Enter the Application Number and Date of Birth.
Step 04: Now click on ‘View Status’.
Application Process for Scheme Benefit:
Step 01: The applicant may visit the Sanman Portal: https://sanman.gujarat.gov.in/﻿
Step 02: On the home page, under the tab ‘Citizen Login’, click on ‘Please Register Here’.
Step 03: Enter your Aadhaar Card Number, select user type, and then enter E-Nirman Card Number.
Step 04: Now, click on ‘Fetch’ & submit the details.
Step 05: After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
Step 06: Now, select the scheme and read the instructions carefully for the selected scheme.
Step 07: Fill out the application form and upload all the relevant documents.
Step 08: Agree with the Rules & Regulations and submit the application form. A confirmation email with the application Number will be sent to the registered email ID.
Post Application Process:
Step 01: After the application form is submitted by the construction worker, the application will be verified and approved by the District Project Manager.
Step 02: After that, the application will be verified and approved by the district inspector and sent to the head office.
Step 03: The State Project Manager of the Head Office will verify the application and send it to the Government Labour Officer (GLO).
Step 04: After that, the application will be verified and approved by the Government Labour Officer of the Head Office and then by the Member Secretary.
Step 05: After approval of the application through DD (Demand Draft) assisted by the Accounts Officer. It will be sent to the Authority.

scheme_name: National Family Benefit Scheme - Gujarat
details: The “National Family Benefit Scheme” is a centrally sponsored scheme implemented in Gujarat by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide social security to households living below the poverty line by offering financial assistance in the event of the death of the primary breadwinner. Under the scheme, a one-time lump sum assistance of ₹20,000 is provided to the widow or unmarried daughter of the deceased, if the primary breadwinner, aged between 18 and 59 years, passes away.
benefits:
A one-time assistance payment of ₹20,000/- is provided to the family of the deceased, whether the death was natural or accidental.
Notes:
The payment is intended for the family, specifically going to the widow or unmarried daughter.
The assistance would be given to every case of death of breadwinner in a family.
Payment of the assistance is made through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the beneficiary’s post or bank account.
This is a one-time assistance payment.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should be a family member of the deceased.
The family should be listed in the Below Poverty Line (BPL) list as determined by the Government of India.
The deceased (male or female) should have been the primary income earner of the family.
The death of the primary income earner should have occurred due to natural or accidental causes.
The age of the deceased at the time of death should have been between 18 and 60 years.
The application should be submitted within two years from the date of death to be eligible under the scheme.
application_process:
Online - Via CSCs
Offline
The interested applicants for assistance can visit the nearest concerned Taluka Jan Seva Kendra- Mamlatdar office with all the mandatory documents.
documents_required:
Identity proof
Proof of family being on the BPL List
Death Certificate of primary income earner (showing natural or accidental cause)
Age proof of deceased/ main income earner (indicating age between 18 and 60 years at time of death)
Bank Account Details
Any other documents as required

details: The “Zupda Vijlikaran Yojana” was launched by the Energy and Petro Chemicals Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to provide access to electricity for Below Poverty Line (BPL) and low-income families across the state. Under this scheme, free household electricity connections are provided to eligible families.
benefits: Free electricity household connection is provided to BPL/Lower income group beneficiary.
eligibility:

The applicant should belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) or low-income family.
The applicant's family annual income should not exceed ₹1,50,000/-.

Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Subdivisional Office.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the Subdivisional Office to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

scheme_name: Shahidvir Kinariwaala Student Security Group Insurance Scheme
category: Banking,Financial Services and Insurance, Education & Learning
details: The "Shahidvir Kinariwaala Student Security Group Insurance Scheme" was launched by the Education Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme provides financial protection in case of accidental death or permanent disability to students or their guardians/heirs to help reduce the burden during unforeseen tragedies. The scheme covers students at universities, Government and Non-Government colleges, Gram Vidyapeeth GBTC colleges, and technical education institutes across the state.
benefits:
Maximum financial assistance of ₹1,00,000/- is provided in case of accidental death or permanent disability. 
Coverage includes accidental death, permanent total disability, loss of two limbs or two eyes, or one limb and one eye (100% coverage).
Partial disability such as loss of one limb or one eye may also be covered at 50%.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a student, guardian or heir of the student. 
The applicant must be studying in any university, government college, non-government college, Gram Vidyapeeth GBTC college, or technical education institute within Gujarat.
The student should have died in an accident or permanent disable. 
The death must have been due to natural cause or suicide.
application_process:
Offline

Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attestted, if required).
Step 3: Duly filled and signed application form along with the documents send it to the office of the commissioner of Higher Education through the college in which you are studying.

scheme_name: Janta Juth Accidental Insurance Scheme
details: "Janta Juth" is a Farmer's Accidental Insurance Scheme launched by the Govt. of Gujarat on 26th January 1996 to provide economic assistance of upto ₹ 2,00,000 to Farmers in case of Death or Disability. It is a 100% state-sponsored scheme. The main objective is to assist the successor of the farmer in case of death or disability due to an accident. In this scheme, insurance premium is paid by the State Government for all farmers. This scheme is implemented under the Gujarat Community Group-Janta Accidental Insurance Scheme from 01/04/08 through Director of Insurance, Gandhinagar. In case of accidental death or permanent disability, all registered farmers, any child (son/daughter) of the farmer and husband/wife of the farmer having age of 5 to 70 years are beneficiaries for the scheme.
benefits:
In case of accidental death or permanent disability 100% assistance of ₹ 2,00,000.
In case of accidental loss of two eyes / two limbs / hand and leg / one eye and one hand or leg 100% assistance of ₹ 2,00,000 (100% full sight loss in case of eye, In case of hand- loss of upper part of the wrist and in case of the leg- totally cut off from the knee).
In case of accidental loss of one eye or one limb 50% assistance of ₹ 1,00,000.
eligibility:
The Deceased or the Person with Permanent Disability Should Be a Registered Farmer (Have Individual or Joint Name Land) or Child of a Registered Farmer (Son or Daughter) or the Husband/ Wife.
The Death or Permanent Disability Must Be Due to an Accident.
The Deceased or the Person with Permanent Disability should be between the age of 5 to 70 years.
The Application Should Be Made Within 150 Days to the Office of the Concerned District Agriculture Officer.
﻿

The sequence of the beneficiary under the scheme in chronological order:
Husband/Wife: In his/her absence
Children Son/Daughter: In their absence
Parent Father/Mother: In their absence
Grandson/Grandaughter: In the absence of 1, 2, 3
Unmarried/ Widow/ Exiled Sister who is dependent and living with the beneficiary
Any heirs declared under the Heirs Act applied to the beneficiary involved in other than above mentioned cases and controversial case
﻿

Note: Suicide or Natural Death Are Not Covered in This Scheme.
application_process:
Offline
The Applicant Should Submit the Application Along With the Relevant Papers in the Prescribed Format to the Concerned District Agricultural Officer, District Panchayat Within 150 Days From the Date of Death or Date of Accidental Disabilities. 
﻿

NOTE 1: In Case of Accidental Death, the Applicant Will Be an Accidental Farmer's Successor and in the Case of Accidental Disability, the Applicant Will Be a Disabled Person Himself. 
NOTE 2: Application Received After 150 Days Will Not Be Eligible.

scheme_name: Mamta Taruni Abhiyan
details:
Details
Under this program, free medical check-ups, iron and folic acid supplements, and deworming medication were provided to adolescent girls between the ages of 10 and 19 years, and pregnant women. The program also aimed to create awareness about menstrual hygiene, family planning, and nutrition among adolescent girls and pregnant women.
The program was implemented through a network of health workers, including Accredited Social Health Activists (ASHAs), Auxiliary Nurse Midwives (ANMs), and Anganwadi workers. The Mamta Taruni Abhiyan was seen as a significant step towards improving maternal and child health in rural areas of Gujarat.
benefits:
Benefits
Access to Health and Wellness Services: The scheme provides access to various health and wellness services, including health check-ups, antenatal care, postnatal care, and nutritional support.
Improved Maternal and Child Health: The scheme aims to improve maternal and child health outcomes by providing timely and quality healthcare services to pregnant women and young girls.
Health Education: The scheme also provides health education and awareness sessions to beneficiaries, which helps them make informed decisions about their health and well-being.
Empowerment of Women and Girls: The scheme aims to empower women and girls by providing them with knowledge and skills related to health and nutrition, which helps them lead healthy and productive life.
Financial Support: The scheme also provides financial support to pregnant women in the form of a cash incentive, which helps them meet their medical and nutritional needs.
eligibility:
Eligibility 
Adolescent Girls: The scheme is open to all adolescent girls between the ages of 10 and 19 years who are residents of Gujarat.
Pregnant Women: The scheme is open to all pregnant women who are residents of Gujarat.
BPL Families: The scheme gives priority to women and girls belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families.
application_process:
Offline
Application Process
Visit the Nearest Health Center: The first step is to visit the nearest healthcare center or hospital that is authorized to provide the benefits and services under the scheme.
Fill the Application Form: The healthcare center will provide an application form for the scheme. The applicant needs to fill the form accurately and completely.
Submit Required Documents: Along with the application form, the applicant needs to submit the required documents such as identification proof, address proof, medical certificate (in case of pregnant women), and income certificate (in case of BPL families).
Get Enrolled: After submitting the application form and the required documents, the healthcare center will verify the information provided by the applicant. If the applicant is found to be eligible, they will be enrolled in the scheme.
Receive Benefits and Services: Once enrolled, the beneficiary can avail the benefits and services under the scheme, which may include health check-ups, antenatal care, postnatal care, nutritional support, health education, and financial support (in case of pregnant women).
documents_required:
List of required documents 
Identification Documents: Beneficiaries must have identification documents such as a birth certificate, Aadhaar card, or any other valid government-issued identity proof.
Medical Certificate: Pregnant women may be required to provide a valid medical certificate indicating their pregnancy status and health condition.
Bank Account Details: Beneficiaries may need to provide their bank account details to receive financial support under the scheme.
Income Certificate: Women and girls belonging to Below Poverty Line (BPL) families may need to provide an income certificate as proof of their eligibility.
Address Proof: Beneficiaries may need to provide an address proof such as a utility bill or a rent agreement to establish their residence in Gujarat.

scheme_name: Satyvadi Raja Harishchandra Marnotar Sahay Yojana
details: The "Satyvadi Raja Harishchandra Marnotar Sahay Yojana" scheme was launched by the Director of Scheduled Caste Welfare, Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat, in the year 2000. Under the scheme, financial assistance is provided to scheduled caste families at the time of the death of a family member to cover funeral expenses, due to their poor economic conditions.
benefits: Under the scheme, scheduled caste families receive financial assistance of ₹5,000/- at the time of a family member's death to cover funeral expenses.
eligibility:
The deceased person as well as the applicant should be a native of Gujarat.
The deceased person as well as the applicant applying should belong to Scheduled Castes only.
The annual income limit of the applicant should be more than ₹6,00,000/- for rural areas and ₹6,00,000/- for urban areas.
The application must be made within a time limit of six months of the death. 
The death registration certificate of the deceased person must be produced. 
Only one person in the family of the deceased can take the benefit at that time.
