scheme_name: COP-37 (General Area) 50% Capital Subsidy Scheme for Project of Taluka & District Co-op. Purchase Sale Union
details: The scheme "COP-37 (General Area) 50% Capital Subsidy Scheme for Project of Taluka & District Co-op. Purchase Sale Union" by the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation, Government of Gujarat, aims to strengthen the financial base of cooperative buying and selling unions in talukas and districts by providing 50% capital assistance with a maximum limit of ₹20,00,000/- for a wide range of agricultural projects. These projects include procurement of inputs like fertilizers and seeds, as well as infrastructure and service improvements such as processing, grading, cleaning, washing, shorting, packaging, branding, storage, and marketing of agricultural produce. By empowering these cooperative institutions, the scheme seeks to ensure better quality services and economic benefits for farmers and primary agricultural credit societies.
benefits:
Financial Assistance: Provides a 50% capital subsidy to cooperative unions, with a maximum limit of ₹20,00,000/-.
Improved Services for Farmers: Enables unions to offer better and more diverse services to farmers, such as providing quality agricultural inputs, procuring produce at support prices, and offering modern processing and storage facilities.
Enhanced Infrastructure: Supports the development of infrastructure like storage godowns, processing units, and marketing channels, which benefits the entire agricultural supply chain.
Increased Market Access: Helps unions establish better marketing and branding of agricultural produce, potentially leading to better prices for farmers' products.
eligibility:
Taluka or District Cooperative Purchase and Sale Associations are eligible under this scheme.
Taluka and District Purchase and Sale Associations should have their own land.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The Taluka or District Cooperative Purchase and Sale Union must prepare a proposal for the intended project.
Step 2: The proposal is to be submitted to the Registrar of Co-operative Societies, Gujarat State, Gandhinagar, through the Deputy Director and District Registrar Co-operative Societies of the respective district.
Step 3: Upon approval from the Department of Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation, the subsidy amount will be disbursed to the union via the District Registrar's office.
Step 4: The assistance will be released in installments based on the level of work completed as per verified reports.
documents_required:
Project Proposal Document.
Land Ownership Proof.
Identity Proof of Union Office Bearers.
Registration Certificate of the Cooperative Union.
Utilization Certificate (post-completion for fund release).
Progress Report (for phased disbursement).
Bank Account Details of the Union.
Approval Letter from District Registrar (for forwarding the proposal).
Any Other Document as Prescribed by the Concerned Authority.

scheme_name: Scheduled Castes Unsalaried Credit Co-operative Society and Credit and Supply Co-operative Scheme for Providing Managerial Assistance and Computer–Furniture to the Society
details: The scheme “Scheduled Castes Unsalaried Credit Co-operative Society and Credit and Supply Co-operative Scheme for Providing Managerial Assistance and Computer–Furniture to the Society” by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to promote the financial inclusion and operational efficiency of Scheduled Caste Savings and Credit Co-operative Societies by offering managerial support and necessary infrastructure. These societies, formed to serve members of the Scheduled Castes through loan and credit services, often lack resources to appoint qualified managers or procure essential office furniture and computer systems. Through this scheme, the government offers financial assistance to help societies pay managerial salaries and purchase basic office infrastructure.
benefits:
Managerial Assistance: A maximum of ₹8,000/- per month is provided for an employee's salary, totaling Rs. 0.96 lakh per society on an annual basis.
Computer and Furniture Assistance: A maximum of Rs. 0.62 lakh per society is provided for the purchase of computers and furniture.
eligibility:
The society must be registered under the Co-operative Act.
The society must be a Scheduled Caste Savings and Credit Co-operative Society.
The society should be formed exclusively by members belonging to the Scheduled Castes.
The applicant society must be registered within the last three financial years prior to the application year.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The interested applicant should prepare the application on plain paper.
Step 2: In the application form, provide all the mandatory details, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the supporting documents to the District Registrar's Office.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted.
documents_required:
Certificate of Registration under the Co-operative Act.
Proof of Scheduled Caste Membership of Society Members.
Society's Financial Records (for verification of operational status).
Quotation/Estimate for Computer and Furniture Procurement (if applying for infrastructure support).
Any other documents as required.

scheme_name: Scheme for Granting Assistance to Processing Units in APMC (General Area)
details: The scheme "Scheme for Granting Assistance to Processing Units in APMC (General Area)" by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to encourage establishment of agro and food processing units in the general areas of Agricultural Produce Market Committees (APMCs) to increase farmers’ income, reduce post-harvest losses, generate employment opportunities, and improve supply chain efficiencies. The scheme provides financial assistance in the form of capital subsidy to co-operatives institutions that set up processing units.
benefits:
Variable Grant Amount: The grant percentage is tailored to the needs of different market committees, offering 25% for 'A' and 'B' class committees and 50% for 'C' and 'D' class committees.
Maximum Assistance: The maximum financial assistance provided under the scheme is capped at ₹1,50,00,000/-, ensuring substantial support for eligible projects.
eligibility:
Co-operative institutions are eligible under this scheme.
The institutions must set up a processing unit in the general area of an APMC.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The interested applicant should prepare the application on plain paper.
Step 2: In the application form, provide all the mandatory details, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attested, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the supporting documents to the District Registrar's Office.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted.

Any other documents as required.

scheme_name: Assistance to Scheduled Castes for Fisheries: Boat Net Gujarat
details:
The scheme "Assistance to Scheduled Castes for Fisheries", with the component of Boat Net, introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and providing employment to Scheduled Caste (SC) individuals involved in inland fisheries. Through this scheme, assistance is provided for boat nets, which are provided to Scheduled Caste fishermen and fish farmers. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries, Government of Gujarat.
Objectives:
To increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to scheduled caste (SC) people involved in inland fisheries activities.
To boost fish farming or fishing activities in freshwater bodies by scheduled caste (SC) fish farmers and fishermen.
Fishing in reservoirs, aquaculture in ponds, and catch from rivers increase fish production and boost inland fisheries through scheduled caste (SC) fish farmers and fishermen.
The main objective is to provide employment opportunities to Scheduled Caste fishermen in the state and increase fish production.
benefits:
Tin Boat-Net Unit: 50% of unit cost or ₹7,500/- (Boat: ₹5,000/-; Net: ₹2,500/-), whichever is less.
F.R.P. (Fibre-Reinforced Plastic) Boat-Net Unit: 50% of unit cost or ₹15,000/- (Boat: ₹12,500/-; Net: ₹2,500/-), whichever is less.
Conditions/Aspects/Criteria Associated: Benefit amount is calculated based on 50% of the unit cost or the fixed maximum amount mentioned above, whichever is less.
Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits: 6 Years
eligibility:
The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) community.
The applicant should be above 18 years of age.
The applicant should be involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant can be an individual, a society, a self-help group, an association, a reservoir monopoly, or a private unit.
The applicant must have a bank account in their own name.
The applicant must have an Aadhar Card/Voter ID/Ration Card/Passport as proof of identity.
﻿

Preference/Reservation:
The scheme specifically benefits Scheduled Caste fishermen under the inland fisheries scheme.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Backyard Ornamental Fish Rearing Unit - Gujarat
details:
The scheme “Backyard Ornamental Fish Rearing Unit” is a component under the “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)” scheme, implemented by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to promote the rearing of ornamental fish and accelerate inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, also increasing inland fish production. The scheme also employs people involved in inland fisheries activities. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
Objective: 
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries. 
Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits:
This scheme provides financial assistance for setting up a Backyard Ornamental Fish Rearing Unit, with a project cost of ₹3,00,000/- per unit.
For the General Category: the scale of assistance is 40% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
For Women, Scheduled Castes (SC), and Scheduled Tribes (ST) categories: the scale of assistance is 60% of the unit cost or 60% of the actual cost, whichever is less.
Mode of Disbursement: 60% of the government contribution is from the Government of India, and 40% is from the State Government.
Frequency of Disbursement: The benefit can be availed once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.
The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Large Biofloc Establishment Culture System - Gujarat
details:
The scheme “Large Biofloc Establishment (50 tanks 4-meter diameter and 1.5-meter height) Culture System” is a component under the “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland)” scheme, implemented by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs, provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities, accelerate inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, and boost inland fisheries. Under this scheme, subsidies for establishing Biofloc and Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS) are provided to individuals, societies, self-help groups, associations, reservoir lease holders, and private entities involved in inland fisheries activities. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
Objective: 
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries. 
Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits:
The scheme provides financial assistance for the establishment of a Large Biofloc Establishment (50 tanks, 4 meter diameter and 1.5 meter height) culture system. The unit cost is ₹50,00,000/- per unit.
For General Category: The scale of assistance is 40% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less, paid as a subsidy.
For Women/Scheduled Caste (S.C.) and Scheduled Tribe (ST): The scale of assistance is 60% of the unit cost or 40% of the actual cost, whichever is less, paid as a subsidy.
Mode of Disbursement: Assistance is paid through a 60% contribution from the Government of India and a 40% contribution from State Governments.
Frequency of Disbursement: The benefit can be reclaimed once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.
The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of the co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhaar Card/Passport as proof of identity.

All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.

scheme_name: Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME): Assistance for Interest Subsidy (Service Sector)
details:
The "Scheme for Assistance to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME)" is an umbrella scheme launched by the Industries and Mines Department, Gujarat. It aims to empower the state's dynamic MSME sector, which significantly contributes to employment, GDP, and exports. Effective from August 7th, 2020 to August 6th, 2025, this initiative supports entrepreneurs and strengthens MSMEs, making them globally competitive.
The component "Assistance for Interest Subsidy (Service Sector)" provides interest subsidies on term loans for new MSMEs in the service sector. The subsidy applies to new machinery, equipment, and furniture/fixtures (excluding land and buildings).
benefits:
Financial Assistance:
Category of Taluka 	Quantum of Incentive
 Category I	8% on Term Loan with a maximum amount of ₹35,00,000/- per annum for a period of 7 years
Category II	7% on Term Loan with a maximum amount of ₹30,00,000/- per annum for a period of 6 years 
Category III & Municipal Corporation Areas	6% on Term Loan with a maximum amount of ₹25,00,000/- per annum for a period of 5 years
﻿

Additional Subsidy: 1% extra interest subsidy for registered startups in the service sector with Udyam/MSME Registration.
eligibility:
The firm must be a new Micro/Small/Medium enterprise.
The enterprise must be in the service sector (listed in Annexure A & B) are eligible.
The term loan must be sanctioned by a recognized Bank/Financial Institution.
The enterprise must apply within one year of loan disbursement or before the commercial production starts.
The enterprise must remain operational for at least 7 years post-subsidy approval.
The enterprise must comply with pollution control measures.
Only one type of investment (Forward Integration, Backward Integration, Diversification, or Expansion) will be eligible for subsidy.
application_process:
Online
Registration Process:
Step-1: Visit the Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP) portal. Click on "New Investor Registration" to access the registration form.
Step-2: Fill in the Registration Form. Click on "Submit". 
Step-3: Verify the E-mail ID using the link sent on your registered E-mail ID.
Step-4: After verification, you will be registered successfully.
﻿

Post-Registration Process:
Step-1: Visit the Investor Facilitation Portal (IFP) portal. Click on "Login".
Step-2: Enter the email address and password you used during registration.
Step-3: Click the "Login" button to access your account.
Step-4: Now fill in the required details and mandatory documents about your business to complete the application process.
﻿

Helpdesk: Contact Here.

scheme_name: Training for Horticulture Skill Development for Self-Employment at Centre of Excellence
details: The scheme "Training for Horticulture Skill Development for Self-Employment at Centre of Excellence" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat,  aims to offer structured training programs ranging from 10 to 30 days, focusing on horticultural skills to enable self-employment. The program is tailored for individuals across all social categories, with subsidized training costs. Beneficiaries gain access to a complete learning experience, including expert sessions, training literature, educational tours, and practical kits.
benefits:
Stipend: ₹250/- per trainee.
Other Facilities: Dining facility, training literature kit, experiential education tour, trainee kit, etc.
Duration of the Training:
Minimum: 10 days
Maximum: 30 days

scheme_name: NEW SWARNIMA (Gopalak Nigam)
category: Business & Entrepreneurship, Social welfare & Empowerment, Women and Child
details:
The New Swarnima Scheme is a financial empowerment initiative by the Gujarat Gopalak Vikas Nigam under the Social Justice and Empowerment Department. It aims to provide 100% loan support to women from the Rabari and Bharwad communities to promote self-employment and entrepreneurship.
Eligible women between the age of 21 and 45 years, with an annual family income up to ₹3 lakh, can receive a loan of up to ₹2 lakh at a subsidized interest rate of 5% per annum to start a business of their choice. The scheme is designed to increase economic independence and livelihood generation among backward class women.
benefits:
100% loan assistance provided to eligible women
Loan amount up to ₹2.00 lakh
Interest rate fixed at 5% per annum
Loan can be used to start a business of the beneficiary’s choice
Meant for economically backward women of specific castes
Encourages entrepreneurship and self-reliance
eligibility:
Be a permanent resident of Gujarat
Be a woman
Belong to the Rabari or Bharwad caste
Belong to a socially and educationally backward class as recognized by the Government of Gujarat
Be between 21 to 45 years of age
Have a total annual family income not exceeding ₹3.00 lakh
Intend to start your own business or self-employment venture using the loan
application_process:
Online
Step 1: Visit the official DAADC online portal.
Step 2: Register/log in using your credentials.
Step 3: Fill out the application form with accurate personal and scheme-specific details.
Step 4: Upload the required documents and submit.

scheme_name: Scheme of Financial Assistance to New Writers of Gujarati Language and Literature for Publishing Original Works of Creative and Fine Literature
details: The "Scheme of Financial Assistance to New Writers of Gujarati Language and Literature for Publishing Original Works of Creative and Fine Literature" has been designed to encourage first-time authors who lack financial means to publish original and high-quality creative works in the Gujarati language. Administered by the Gujarat Sahitya Academy, the scheme provides grants to cover partial printing costs for eligible manuscripts. Works eligible for assistance include novels, short stories, essays, poetry, and plays that promote cultural awareness, national unity, and intellectual development. The manuscript must be original, unpublished, and reviewed positively by expert evaluators.
benefits:
Financial Assistance:
For manuscripts with up to 112 pages: Up to ₹10,000/-.
For manuscripts exceeding 112 pages: Up to ₹15,000/-.
Printing Size Guidelines:
For “Crown” size: ₹2,100/- (paperback), ₹2,200/- (hardcover).
For “Demy” size: ₹2,300/- (paperback), ₹2,500/- (hardcover).
For “Double Crown” size: ₹2,700/- (paperback), ₹2,800/- (hardcover).
Note: The lower of the printing cost or the above ceiling will be granted as assistance.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a first-time writer in the Gujarati language and literature.
The manuscript must be an original literary work such as a novel, play, essay, poetry collection, or autobiography.
The manuscript must not be edited work or related to non-literary subjects.
The applicant must not have received more than five grants from any language academy affiliated with Gujarat Sahitya Academy.
The manuscript must be typed, bound, paginated, and submitted in two copies.
The applicant must not change the title, content, or format after submission without written permission from the Academy.
Note: Only one application per financial year is allowed per author.

scheme_name: Best Book Award
details: The Best Book Award scheme by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, promotes high-quality literary creation and recognition of literary talent. It awards the best books published each calendar year in various languages through the Gujarat Sahitya Akademi. Under this scheme, the first prize includes ₹11,000 along with a certificate and shield; the second prize includes ₹7,000 with a certificate and shield; and the third prize includes ₹5,000, also with a certificate and shield. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat Sahitya Akademi.
benefits:
Monetary Benefits:

Commemorative Shield

The applicant’s book should be written in a language recognised by the Gujarat Sahitya Academy.
Note: 
Prizes are awarded to the best books in the respective language published by Cha Sahitya Academy every calendar year.
The scheme is applicable to Gujarat as well as across the country.

scheme_name: MICRO FINANCE (Gopalak Nigam)
details:
This microfinance scheme aims to empower small entrepreneurs from the Rabari and Bharwad castes by offering affordable loans with minimal beneficiary contribution. Designed to support income-generating activities, the scheme helps beneficiaries fund small businesses with 95% of the project cost covered by the corporation, while only 5% is required as their own contribution.
The maximum loan offered under the scheme is ₹1.25 lakh, with a subsidized interest rate of 5% per annum. The scheme is intended to improve economic participation and entrepreneurship among socially and educationally backward communities in Gujarat.
benefits:
Loan assistance up to ₹1.25 lakh for small businesses
Interest rate fixed at 5% per annum
95% loan coverage by the corporation
Only 5% contribution needed from the applicant
Promotes economic independence and small-scale entrepreneurship
eligibility:
The applicant must be a permanent resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must be a woman or man belonging to the Rabari or Bharwad caste.
The applicant must belong to a socially and educationally backward class as recognized by the Gujarat government.
The applicant must be between 21 and 45 years of age.
The applicant’s annual family income must not exceed ₹3.00 lakh.
The applicant must be planning to start or expand a small business.

scheme_name: Foreign Study Loan Scheme (GUEEDC)
details: The scheme provides financial loan assistance to students from the unreserved category to pursue higher education abroad. Loans up to ₹15 lakh are granted at simple interest of 4% per annum, with flexible repayment options. Security and repayment conditions depend on the loan amount. This scheme aims to support meritorious, economically weaker students for global education opportunities.
benefits:
Loan Amount: Up to ₹15.00 lakh
Interest Rate: Simple interest at 4% per annum
Collateral:
Up to ₹7.50 lakh: Property valued at 1.5 times the loan amount
Above ₹7.50 lakh: Property equal to the loan amount
Loan Repayment:
Up to ₹5 lakh: repay in 5 years after 1-year moratorium
Above ₹5 lakh: repay in 6 years after 1-year moratorium
﻿

Borrower can pre-pay without penalty
eligibility:
The applicant must be a citizen of India and a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must belong to the Unreserved category.
The applicant must have passed Class 12 with 60% or more marks.
The applicant must be seeking admission to MBBS, Master’s, PG Diploma, or equivalent programs abroad.
The applicant’s annual family income must be ₹6.00 lakh or less.

scheme_name: Scheme to Provide Monthly Financial Assistance to Elderly and Eligible Writers
details: The "Scheme to Provide Monthly Financial Assistance to Elderly and Eligible Writers" is a welfare initiative implemented by the Gujarat Sahitya Academy to provide financial security to elderly writers who have significantly contributed to Gujarati, Hindi, Sanskrit, Sindhi, Urdu, or Kutchi literature. The scheme offers monthly assistance to writers who are 60 years of age or older, have no steady pension or major income source, and are economically disadvantaged.
benefits: Monthly Financial Support: Eligible elderly writers receive monthly financial assistance as specified by the authority.
eligibility:
The applicant must be 60 years or older.
The applicant must have made a significant literary contribution (e.g., authored books, received awards, or provided literary service).
The applicant must belong to one of the recognized language academies: Gujarati, Hindi, Sanskrit, Sindhi, Urdu, or Kutchi.
The applicant’s annual family income must be below ₹1,20,000 in rural areas and below ₹1,50,000 in urban areas, certified by the Mamlatdar.
The applicant must not be receiving any pension.
The applicant must not already be receiving financial support under similar schemes.

scheme_name: Scheme to Provide Financial Assistance for Translation of Scholarly Works
details: The "Scheme to Provide Financial Assistance for Translation of Scholarly Works" by the Sports, Youth and Cultural Activities Department, Government of Gujarat, promotes the translation of significant literary, cultural, and scholarly works to and from the Gujarati language to foster national integration and cross-cultural dialogue. The scheme is implemented by the Gujarat Sahitya Academy and offers financial support to individuals and institutions for translating original works from other Indian languages into Gujarati or vice versa. The scheme covers a wide range of genres, including novels, dramas, stories, essays, one-act plays, poetry, criticism, and research-based works.
benefits:

The translation must be from one Indian language into Gujarati, or from Gujarati into another Indian language.
The original author’s or publisher’s written permission for translation must be obtained and attached.
The manuscript must be unpublished and originally translated by the applicant.
Only one application per financial year is allowed.
Applicants who have received financial assistance five times or more under Sahitya Academy schemes are ineligible.

scheme_name: Provide Assistance to Farmers Bringing Goods to Market Committees for Cost of Goods Transportation (Tribal)
details: The scheme "Provide Assistance to Farmers Bringing Goods to Market Committees for Cost of Goods Transportation (Tribal)" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to promote the participation of tribal farmers in formal agricultural markets by reimbursing transportation costs incurred while bringing agricultural goods to market committees. The primary objective is to reduce the financial burden of logistics on tribal farmers and thereby enhance their income through better market access and fair price realization.
benefits: Financial Assistance: ₹500/- per tonne of agricultural produce transported. Assistance is available for up to 10 tonnes per farmer, which amounts to a maximum of ₹5,000/-.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a tribal farmer.
The applicant should be residing in a tribal area of Gujarat.
The applicant should bring their agricultural produce to a registered market committee (APMC) located in the tribal area.
The applicant should be the owner or cultivator of the agricultural goods transported.

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY-Inland): Inputs for Freshwater Aquaculture - Gujarat
details:
The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY – Inland)" with the component of Inputs for Freshwater Aquaculture (Including Composite Fish Culture, Scampi, Pagasius, Tilapia) introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% from the Government of Gujarat.
﻿

Objective: 
Increase fish production by rearing fish in reservoirs and provide employment to people involved in inland fisheries activities
Assistance is provided for accelerating inland fish farming activities in freshwater bodies, increasing inland fish production and boosting inland fisheries. 
Except for water bodies or ponds, in less land, less water, Biofloc with source as well, Recirculating Aquaculture System (RAS). By creating fish tanks with this method, more fish production can be obtained in less land and less water. If more beneficiaries get the benefit of the subsidy of this scheme, more fish production can be achieved with less water sources and less land.
Inland fish production capacity can be increased by the establishment of freshwater hatcheries.
benefits:
The unit cost of the project for Inputs for Freshwater Aquaculture (Including Composite Fish Culture, Scampi, Pangasius, and Tilapia) is ₹4,00,000/-.
Beneficiaries in the general category receive 40% of the unit cost as assistance.
Women, Scheduled Caste (SC), and Scheduled Tribe (ST) beneficiaries receive 60% of the unit cost as assistance.
The subsidy is jointly funded: 60% by the Government of India and 40% by the Government of Gujarat.
Mode of Disbursement: Assistance is paid with a 60% contribution from the Government of India and a 40% contribution from the State Governments (specifically, the Government of Gujarat as mentioned in the provided text).
Validity: A minimum time limit of 1 year is set for reclaiming benefits.
eligibility:
The applicant must be an individual, society, self-help group, association, reservoir lease holder, or private entity involved in inland fisheries activities.
The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
The applicant must have their own bank account.
The applicant must have their own private land or a minimum 10-year lease agreement for land to avail construction-related assistance on land. In case of co-owners, the consent form of co-owners must be submitted.
The applicant must have a Voter ID/Ration Card/Aadhar Card/Passport as proof of identity.
﻿

Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:
All categories (General, Women, Scheduled Caste, and Scheduled Tribe) will receive benefits under the Inland Schemes.
Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe beneficiaries are required to have a Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe certificate to avail benefits under the inland components.

scheme_name: AGR-4 Scheduled Castes Sub-Plan for Distribution of Seeds and Fertilizers Input Kits at Subsidised Rates to Scheduled Caste Cultivators

Reduced Cultivation Costs: By providing subsidized inputs, the scheme lowers the overall cost of cultivation, helping to improve the economic viability of farming for SC cultivators.

The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Caste category.

scheme_name: Whale Shark Conservation Programme
details: Launched in 2004, the "Whale Shark Conservation Programme" by the Forests and Environment Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to tackle the problem of incidental catch and conserve the magnificent Whale Shark species, ensuring its protection along the Gujarat coast. The scheme provides full legal protection to Whale Sharks, listing them under Schedule-I of the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972 since May 28, 2001, marking it as the first fish protected by Indian law. It also features a compensation scheme for fishermen whose nets are damaged during rescue operations, and introduced a self-documentation process for claims in 2012. The fishermen must actively participate in rescue operations by reporting trapped Whale Sharks and assisting in their release. The scheme is implemented by the Forests and Environment Department. Applications for compensation under this scheme are accepted through reporting incidents to the forest range office or by self-documentation.
benefits:
Compensation for Damaged Nets
Fishermen are paid compensation not exceeding ₹25,000/- (twenty-five thousand rupees) in lieu of nets that are damaged or cut during rescue operations involving trapped Whale Sharks.
Conditions for Benefit Availment/Disbursement
The rescue operation must be documented through videography or photography for compensation to be paid.
The fishermen must take photographs of trapped Whale Sharks, release them on the spot, and then submit this information and photographs after returning to the coast to receive their compensation.
eligibility:
The applicant must be a fisherman, particularly from the Kharva and Koli communities residing in the coastal talukas of Veraval, Sutrapada, and Mangrol in Junagadh district.
The applicant's fishing net must incur damage or require cutting during an operation to rescue a trapped Whale Shark.
The applicant, or the rescue team involved, must ensure the rescue operation is thoroughly documented through either videography or photography.
The applicant, under the self-documentation scheme, must photograph the trapped Whale Shark, release it on the spot, and then submit these photographs along with incident details to the Forests and Environment Department after returning to the sea coast.
application_process:
Offline
Step 1: Transmitting Information to Land 
The crew of a fishing boat or trawler must transmit information about a Whale Shark getting trapped in their nets to personnel on land.
Step 2: Forwarding Information to Forest Range Office
This transmitted information is subsequently forwarded to the respective forest range office, which is responsible for initiating rescue efforts.
Step 3: Organizing a Rescue Operation
Upon receiving the information, the staff at the forest range office organizes a comprehensive rescue operation by gathering available beat guards, local labourers, fisheries personnel, a boat, and other necessary resources.
Step 4: Releasing the Whale Shark
A designated rescue boat then departs for the site where the Whale Shark is trapped. Upon reaching the location, the staff, volunteers, and workmen proceed to cut the nets and safely release the fish.
Step 5: Documenting the Operation
The entire rescue operation, from the sighting to the release of the Whale Shark, is thoroughly documented through either videography or photography.
Step 6: Receiving Compensation
Following the successful and documented rescue operation, compensation is paid to the fishermen in accordance with the established guidelines for damaged nets.
Step 7: Self-Documentation and Claiming Compensation (Option Since 2012)
Fishermen have the option, introduced since 2012, to personally document the incident by taking photographs of the trapped Whale Shark and releasing it on the spot. After returning to the sea coast, they are required to submit this information and the photographs to the Forests and Environment Department to claim their compensation.
﻿

Help & Support / Grievance Redressal 
For any assistance or to address grievances related to the Whale Shark Conservation Programme, applicants can utilize the official contact channels provided by the Forests and Environment Department, Gujarat. 
General FAQs and a Forest Helpline Number are available on the department's website for citizen queries and support.
Contact Details
Specific contact details for Forest Officers are available on the main website of the Principal Chief Conservator of Forest & Head of the Forest Force, Government of Gujarat. 
The Gujarat Biodiversity Board, a partner in conservation, can be reached at 5th Floor, Aranya Bhavan, Sector-10A, Gandhinagar, with a phone number: (079) 23257950.
Conclusion
Upon completing the compensation claim process and submitting all necessary documentation, applicants are strongly advised to retain a copy of their submitted information and any acknowledgment receipts for future reference and tracking purposes. 
This practice helps in verifying submissions and facilitating any follow-up queries regarding the compensation claim.
documents_required:
Proof of Identification as a Fisherman.
Proof of Damaged Fishing Nets.
Photographs of the Trapped Whale Shark (Required for both self-documentation and Department-led rescue claims).
Photographs of the Whale Shark Being Released (Required for both self-documentation and Department-led rescue claims).
Videography of the Rescue Operation.
Any Other Relevant Information Regarding the Trapping and Release of the Whale Shark.

scheme_name: Assistance for Establishment of On farm Storage for Sorting-Grading
details: The scheme "Assistance for Establishment of On-Farm Storage for Sorting-Grading" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat aims to reduce post-harvest losses in horticultural crops and improve value addition by providing financial assistance to farmers for establishing on-farm infrastructure, including sorting, grading, packing, and primary value addition. The support includes both construction-related infrastructure and modern equipment/machinery for on-farm operations.
benefits:
For Construction of On-Farm Infrastructure: Financial aid will be 50% of the unit cost or ₹1,50,000/- per unit, whichever is lower.
For Machinery or Equipment Purchase: Financial aid of 50% of the actual cost, subject to a maximum of ₹50,000/- per unit, whichever is less.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should be a farmer.

scheme_name: Assistance for Area Expansion of Vegetable Crops through Hybrid Seed
details: The scheme "Assistance for Area Expansion of Vegetable Crops through Hybrid Seed" by the Agriculture and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to support vegetable farmers in enhancing their cultivation areas using hybrid seeds and to promote natural farming practices. The financial assistance provided under the scheme helps reduce input costs and encourages environmentally sustainable agricultural methods among the farming community.
benefits:
Subsidy Assistance for Area Expansion Using Hybrid Seeds: 40% of unit cost or up to ₹20,000/- per hectare, whichever is less.
Promotion of Natural Farming in Vegetable Crops: Maximum Assistance: ₹20,000/- per hectare.
eligibility:
The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should be a farmer.
The applicant should be engaged in vegetable crop cultivation.
